examples.english: I had to move because I couldn't afford the rent any more.
examples.chinese: 我得搬家，因为我再也付不起房租了。

examples.english: How can you afford to eat in restaurants all the time?
examples.chinese: 常常在餐厅吃饭，你怎能负担得起？

examples.english: He's finally earning more, so he can afford to have a holiday this year.
examples.chinese: 他终于赚得多些了，今年能够有钱去度假了。

examples.english: They say they will have all the money on Friday, but I don't think they'll be able to pay.
examples.chinese: 他们声称星期五所有的钱就能到手，但我认为他们还是没有能力付款。

examples.english: Some people just aren't able to pay the tax.
examples.chinese: 一些人就是缴纳不起税款。

examples.english: Many people use credit as a way of buying goods they are not able to pay for.
examples.chinese: 许多人赊账购买他们无力支付的商品。

centers.english: to pay someone to do something

examples.english: How much do they pay you?
examples.chinese: 他们付给你多少钱?

examples.english: They still haven't paid her for the work she did last year.
examples.chinese: 她去年干的活他们还没有支付报酬。

examples.english: Did she pay you for taking care of her kids?
examples.chinese: 你照顾她的孩子，她给你报酬吗?

examples.english: We get paid at the end of every month.
examples.chinese: 我们每月底领薪水。

examples.english: Ziedler was ready to pay her $2000 a week.
examples.chinese: 齐德勒准备每周付她2,000美元。

examples.english: If you pay someone to work in your house, you have to pay Social Security taxes on the wages.
examples.chinese: 如果雇人在你家里干活，你必须按工资缴纳社会保障税。

examples.english: Jobs in areas that use mathematical skills, such as computer programming, tend to pay well.
examples.chinese: 电脑编程等用到数学技能的工作往往薪酬很高。

examples.english: Many Britons receive four or five weeks of paid holiday a year.
examples.chinese: 许多英国人每年有四五周的带薪假期。

examples.english: He has a very well-paid job in finance.
examples.chinese: 他有一份财务方面的高薪工作。

examples.english: a highly paid executive
examples.chinese: 一名高薪管理人员

examples.english: Most badly paid jobs are done by women.
examples.chinese: 许多薪酬低的工作是妇女干的。

examples.english: The job is exhausting and badly paid.
examples.chinese: 这个工作既累人薪水又低。

examples.english: Did you tip the waiter?
examples.chinese: 你给服务生小费了吗？

examples.english: It's usual to tip about 15% in restaurants.
examples.chinese: 在餐厅里一般要付15%左右的小费。

examples.english: A service charge is included on the bill, so tipping isn't necessary.
examples.chinese: 服务费已包含在账单里了，因此没有必要付小费。

examples.english: I didn't want to lend Terry my car, but he said he'd make it worth my while.
examples.chinese: 我不想把车子借给特里，但他说他会酬谢我的。

examples.english: The basketball federation in Kuwait offered him a coaching job, and made it worth his while.
examples.chinese: 科威特的篮球协会给他提供了一份教练的工作，薪水也没亏待他。

examples.english: Do you really think the cops can't be bought off?
examples.chinese: 你真的以为无法买通警察吗？

examples.english: The management has been trying to buy off union activists with substantial pay offers.
examples.chinese: 管理层试图以高薪收买工会中的积极分子。

centers.english: to pay someone to do something dishonest or illegal
centers.chinese: 付钱叫某人做不诚实或违法的事

examples.english: Santo was convicted of bribing tax inspectors in Italy.
examples.chinese: 桑托在意大利行贿税务稽查员，被判有罪。

examples.english: He bribed a guard to smuggle a note out of the prison.
examples.chinese: 他贿赂一名狱警，将一张字条偷运出监狱。

examples.english: Judges are bribed or threatened into making decisions favorable to drug traffickers.
examples.chinese: 法官们或被收买或受到威胁，作出有利于毒贩的裁决。

examples.english: The judge was accused of accepting bribes.
examples.chinese: 那名法官被控收受贿赂。

examples.english: There was widespread bribery and corruption in the police department.
examples.chinese: 警察部普遍存在受贿和贪污现象。

examples.english: Top executives received millions of dollars in kickbacks.
examples.chinese: 高级行政人员收受了数百万美元的回扣。

examples.english: He offered me $20,000 as a kickback if I'd push through a $500,000 loan.
examples.chinese: 他提出，如果我帮助他得到50万美元的贷款，他就给我2万美元作为回扣。

examples.english: He denies accepting backhanders, though he admits being offered them.
examples.chinese: 他否认受贿，但承认有人向他行贿。

examples.english: George gave the bouncer a bung, and we got into the nightclub.
examples.chinese: 乔治给了门卫一点钱，我们便进了那家夜总会。

centers.english: to pay back money that you owe someone

examples.english: I'll pay you back tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 我明天把欠你的钱还给你。

examples.english: How are you going to pay back all that money?
examples.chinese: 你打算怎样偿还那一大笔钱?

examples.english: He's paid back about half of what he owes us.
examples.chinese: 他欠我们的钱已经还了一半左右。

examples.english: After graduation, the student must begin to pay back the loan.
examples.chinese: 毕业后，那个学生必须开始偿还贷款。

examples.english: The loan has to be repaid within two years.
examples.chinese: 贷款必须在两年内偿还。

examples.english: There were doubts about the country's ability to repay the debt.
examples.chinese: 人们对该国的偿债能力心存疑虑。

examples.english: My parents lent me the money to buy a car, and I repaid them over the next year.
examples.chinese: 父母借钱给我买车，我第二年把钱还给了他们。

examples.english: The mortgage will be paid off over twenty-five years.
examples.chinese: 那笔抵押借款要在25年内还清。

examples.english: He paid off the loan six months early.
examples.chinese: 他提前六个月把贷款还清了。

examples.english: The country hopes to pay all its debts off within twenty years.
examples.chinese: 该国希望在20年内还清债务。

examples.english: If they don't pay up we will take legal action.
examples.chinese: 如果他们迟迟不肯付钱，我们就采取法律行动。

examples.english: The company paid up eventually, but only after repeated threats and reminders.
examples.chinese: 该公司反复受到警告和提醒，最后总算付钱了。

examples.english: Please settle this account within two weeks.
examples.chinese: 请在两周内把这笔账结清。

examples.english: I settled the bill and left the restaurant.
examples.chinese: 我结账离开了餐馆。

examples.english: This cheque should clear my overdraft.
examples.chinese: 这张支票应该能帮我还清透支的款项。

examples.english: We're hoping that we can clear all our debts by the end of the year.
examples.chinese: 我们希望今年年底能把所有的欠债还清。

expressions.text: give somebody their money back/give somebody a refund

examples.english: We'll give you a refund if you're not entirely satisfied.
examples.chinese: 如果你不完全满意，我们将如数退还。

examples.english: It's the wrong size. Do you think they'll give me my money back?
examples.chinese: 尺寸不对。你认为他们会把钱退给我吗?

examples.english: You can't have a refund unless you bring us the receipt.
examples.chinese: 带收据来才能退款。

examples.english: I think I should get a pretty big tax refund this year.
examples.chinese: 我认为今年能拿到一大笔退税款。

examples.english: The fee will be refunded upon presentation of the receipt.
examples.chinese: 出示收据就能退回这笔费用。

examples.english: Pay for the hotel room when you leave, and the company will reimburse you later.
examples.chinese: 你走的时候把酒店房费付掉，然后向公司报销。

examples.english: He wouldn't let me reimburse him for the cost of his journey.
examples.chinese: 他不让我偿还他的旅费。

examples.english: We pay for any repairs that need doing to the house, and are reimbursed by the landlord.
examples.chinese: 我们支付所有必需的房屋修缮费，然后房东把这笔钱补还给我们。

examples.english: The Ford Citibank credit card offers a 5 percent rebate on the purchase of a new Ford car or truck.
examples.chinese: 用福特花旗银行信用卡购买新福特汽车或卡车可获5%退款。

examples.english: We were delighted to hear that we were entitled to a tax rebate of over £1000.
examples.chinese: 听到我们可得到一千多英镑的退税款消息后，大家都很高兴。

centers.english: to pay for something before you receive it
centers.chinese: 收到东西前支付全额或部分款项

examples.english: Customs fees are paid in advance as part of your airline ticket.
examples.chinese: 海关手续费包含在机票内预先支付。

examples.english: Don't pay cash in advance for any service.
examples.chinese: 获得服务之前不要预付现金。

examples.english: I paid the builders £100 up front and will give them the rest when the job's finished.
examples.chinese: 我预付给建筑工100英镑，待工作完成后再把余款付清。

examples.english: We've had so many unpaid bills that we've started to demand payment up front.
examples.chinese: 坏账太多，所以我们已开始要求预先付款了。

examples.english: We saved enough money to make a down payment on a house.
examples.chinese: 我们存的钱足以支付买房时的首期付款了。

examples.english: He borrowed money from his family to put a down payment on a truck.
examples.chinese: 他向家人借钱付订金买卡车。

examples.english: We've put a deposit on a round-the-world tour.
examples.chinese: 我们已支付了环球旅行的订金。

examples.english: The Center has helped several poor families pay the deposit on a better apartment.
examples.chinese: 该中心曾帮助几户穷苦家庭支付订金购买好一点的公寓。

examples.english: I've put £200 down on a new bedroom carpet.
examples.chinese: 我已付了200英镑作为购买卧室新地毯的订金。

examples.english: Greg's parents are going to give us some money to put down on a car.
examples.chinese: 格雷格的父母打算给我们一些钱以支付购买汽车的订金。

examples.english: I'd like to put this sweater on layaway, please.
examples.chinese: 我想预付部分货款订购这件毛衣。

centers.english: to buy something and pay for it later

examples.english: Most people have to make major purchases on credit.
examples.chinese: 大部分人购买昂贵商品得分期付款。

examples.english: In 2001, 56% of new cars were bought on credit.
examples.chinese: 2001年，56%的新车是分期付款购买的。

examples.english: Can I put it on the slate, and I'll pay at the end of the week?
examples.chinese: 先给我记在账上，本周末我会付清，可以吗？

centers.english: to pay money to someone because they have suffered an injury, loss, damage etc
centers.chinese: 付钱给某人，因为其承受了伤害、损失、破坏等

examples.english: People are entitled to be compensated fully whenever they are injured by others’ carelessness.
examples.chinese: 因他人的疏忽而受伤的人都有权要求赔偿全部损失。

examples.english: No amount of money can compensate for my father's death.
examples.chinese: 父亲的死不是金钱能够补偿的。

examples.english: The workers have still not been compensated for their loss of wages.
examples.chinese: 工人们还没得到工资损失的赔偿。

examples.english: Survivors of the air crash were paid $10000 each in damages.
examples.chinese: 每一位空难的幸存者都得到了10,000美元的赔偿金。

examples.english: Damages of £2500 were awarded by the court.
examples.chinese: 法庭判决，必须支付2,500英镑的赔偿金。

examples.english: The government cannot take private property for public use without compensation.
examples.chinese: 政府征用私人财产必须赔偿。

examples.english: His employers paid him $5000 compensation for his broken leg.
examples.chinese: 他因腿部骨折而得到雇主支付的5,000美元的赔偿金。

examples.english: See if you can get some compensation from the airline for your lost baggage.
examples.chinese: 看看你是否能因行李丢失而从航空公司得到些赔偿。

centers.english: to provide money for someone else to live on
centers.chinese: 提供某人维持生活所需的钱

examples.english: When she was unemployed it was very difficult to provide for her children.
examples.chinese: 她失业期间，很难维持孩子的生计。

examples.english: A life insurance policy enables you to provide for your family after your death.
examples.chinese: 一份人寿保险能够使你在死后继续有钱养活家人。

examples.english: He left his family well-provided for.
examples.chinese: 他留给家人足够的生活费。

examples.english: He has a wife and two children to support.
examples.chinese: 他要养活妻子和两个孩子。

examples.english: A lot of people can barely earn enough to support themselves, let alone their families.
examples.chinese: 许多人挣的钱几乎无法养活自己，更不要说家人了。

examples.english: My parents didn't have to support me when I was at college because I received a grant.
examples.chinese: 我上大学时父母不必供养我，因为我有助学金。

examples.english: Maintenance will be paid until the child reaches 18 or leaves full-time education.
examples.chinese: 抚养费一直要付到孩子满18岁或完成全日制教育为止。

examples.english: He had been paying child support for his two children since 1985.
examples.chinese: 自1985年起他一直在支付两个孩子的抚养费。

examples.english: The judge ordered McFadden to pay alimony of $2,400 a month.
examples.chinese: 法官判决麦克法登每月支付2，400美元生活费。

centers.english: to provide money to help someone do something
centers.chinese: 提供钱以帮助某人做某事

examples.english: Many companies subsidize meals for their workers.
examples.chinese: 许多公司给员工膳食津贴。

examples.english: a government-subsidized health service
examples.chinese: 一家政府资助的医疗机构

examples.english: The city council subsidizes the local orchestra.
examples.chinese: 市议会资助地方管弦乐队。

examples.english: The new league will be sponsored by Pepsi Cola.
examples.chinese: 新联盟将由百事可乐赞助。

examples.english: The bank is sponsoring an art exhibition.
examples.chinese: 该银行将赞助一次画展。

examples.english: Sponsors’ corporate logos are placed on the boards surrounding the field.
examples.chinese: 赞助商的公司标志放在场地四周的广告板上。

examples.english: Both schools and industry will be involved in funding the new training projects.
examples.chinese: 学校和业界将共同为新的培训计划提供经费。

examples.english: a charity funded by private donations
examples.chinese: 一家由私人捐款资助的慈善机构

examples.english: The state should fund the arts for the benefit of us all.
examples.chinese: 为了我们大众的利益，国家应该提供经费资助艺术。

examples.english: The competition is being bankrolled by a New York businessman and computer enthusiast.
examples.chinese: 这项比赛由一位纽约商人兼电脑爱好者资助。

examples.english: Ed Bass, a millionaire from Texas, bankrolled the Biosphere project.
examples.chinese: 德州百万富翁埃德·巴斯资助了生物圈计划。

examples.english: The British government has agreed to underwrite the project with a grant of £5 million.
examples.chinese: 英国政府已同意拨款500万英镑资助该计划。

examples.english: The venture was underwritten by several companies.
examples.chinese: 该风险项目由数家公司提供资助并承担经济责任。

examples.english: Small investors and large companies have both put money into the exhibition.
examples.chinese: 小投资者和大公司都对这次展览会进行了投资。

examples.english: He put all his money into a dotcom company that later failed.
examples.chinese: 他把所有资金都投在一家网络公司上，后来那家公司倒闭了。

examples.english: The government uses money from taxes to finance higher education.
examples.chinese: 政府从税收中抽出一部分资助高等教育。

examples.english: She gave swimming lessons to finance her stay in Australia.
examples.chinese: 为了攒钱支付在澳大利亚的费用，她开班教人游泳。

examples.english: It's a great show, but he can't find anyone to back it.
examples.chinese: 这是一项出色的演出，但他无法找到赞助商。

examples.english: The bank is eager to back business ideas by local people.
examples.chinese: 这家银行积极支持当地人的经营设想。

examples.english: Many biotech companies are not yet profitable, as they continue to pour money into research and trials.
examples.chinese: 许多生物科技公司一直把大量资金投入在研究和试验中，但还没赢利。

examples.english: The way to solve the education crisis is not necessarily to throw money at it.
examples.chinese: 解决教育危机不一定要花大钱。

examples.english: The Republicans see him as a liberal whose only solution to the nation's problems was to throw money at them.
examples.chinese: 共和党人将他视为自由主义者，他解决该国问题的唯一方法是花钱。

centers.english: an amount of money that is paid

examples.english: Your first payment is due on July 16th.
examples.chinese: 你应于7月16日支付第一笔款项。

examples.english: The family spends about $1,800 a month on their mortgage payments.
examples.chinese: 那家人每月大概要还1,800美元的抵押贷款。

examples.english: He makes monthly payments into his ex-wife's bank account.
examples.chinese: 他每月将款项打进前妻的银行账户。

examples.english: They have a monthly car payment of £220.
examples.chinese: 他们为汽车每月付款220英镑。

examples.english: To avoid penalties, pay the installments by the December 10 and April 10 dates.
examples.chinese: 为避免罚款，在12月10日和4月10日将款项分期付清。

examples.english: I borrowed $2000, which was to be paid back in monthly installments of $250.
examples.chinese: 我借了2,000美元，以每月支付250美元的方式分期偿还。

examples.english: You can pay me in instalments if you can't afford to give me all the money back in one go.
examples.chinese: 如果你无力一次付清我所有的钱，可以分期支付。

examples.english: Most stores ask for a small deposit if they are to keep goods for you.
examples.chinese: 大多数店家替你保留商品前都会要求先支付小额订金。

examples.english: Yes, we have plenty of rooms available, but you'll have to pay a $20 deposit.
examples.chinese: 是的，我们有很多房间空着，但你必须先支付20美元的订金。

examples.english: She's paid the deposit on a new bed.
examples.chinese: 她已支付了新床的订金。

examples.english: Would you mind leaving a deposit? You can collect the picture when it's ready next week.
examples.chinese: 你可以留下订金吗？下周画完成后你就可以取走了。

examples.english: For a 40 percent down payment on a new car, the company will arrange a no-interest loan.
examples.chinese: 购买新车的首付款达到40%的话，该公司将提供免息贷款。

expressions.text: make a down payment on something

examples.english: We almost have enough to make a down payment on the house.
examples.chinese: 我们的钱差不多够支付那幢房子的首期款项了。

examples.english: The boy carried my suitcases up to my room and then stood waiting for a tip.
examples.chinese: 侍应生把手提箱拎到楼上我的房间里，然后站在那里等我付小费。

examples.english: A 15% tip is considered usual if the service was good.
examples.chinese: 要是服务周到，15%的小费被认为是正常的。

examples.english: We finished our lunch and left a tip on the table for the waiter.
examples.chinese: 我们吃完午饭后，把给侍者的小费放在了桌子上。

centers.english: money that you have to pay as a punishment
centers.chinese: 作为一种惩罚必须支付的钱

examples.english: I got a fine for parking on a double yellow line.
examples.chinese: 我把车停在画了双黄线的路上而被罚款了。

examples.english: There are heavy fines for drink-driving. You might even go to prison.
examples.chinese: 酒后驾车要被重罚,甚至可能要入狱。

examples.english: He got a $75 fine for speeding.
examples.chinese: 他因超速驾驶被罚款75美元。

examples.english: If you're going into town, will you go and pay my library fines for me please?
examples.chinese: 如果你进城，能否替我去交一下图书馆的罚金？

examples.english: Stores will be fined for selling cigarettes or tobacco to minors.
examples.chinese: 商店向未成年人出售香烟或烟草要被罚款。

examples.english: The company was fined $1.6 million for breaking environmental regulations.
examples.chinese: 那家公司因违反环保条例被罚款160万美元。

examples.english: You will be fined for any lost library books.
examples.chinese: 丢失图书馆的书要被罚款。

centers.english: a piece of paper that shows how much you must pay
centers.chinese: 表明必须支付金额的单据

examples.english: Can I have the bill, please?
examples.chinese: 请给我结账。

examples.english: We've just had a huge telephone bill.
examples.chinese: 我们刚收到一张巨额电话费账单。

examples.english: They left the hotel without paying the bill.
examples.chinese: 他们没付账就离开了酒店。

examples.english: The garage sent me a bill for £400.
examples.chinese: 修车行寄给我一张400英镑的账单。

examples.english: The bill for the meal came to $75, including wine.
examples.chinese: 这一餐包括酒水总共75美元。

examples.english: A waiter came over and handed me the check.
examples.chinese: 服务员过来给我账单。

examples.english: Let me pay the check.
examples.chinese: 我来付账吧。

examples.english: The bride's father paid the tab for the party.
examples.chinese: 新娘的父亲付了宴会的账单。

examples.english: He ordered dinner and asked for it to be put on his tab.
examples.chinese: 他点了菜，要求记在他的账上。

examples.english: In just two days, she'd run up a bar tab of $175.
examples.chinese: 才两天，她就积欠了酒吧175美元账款。

examples.english: You will find the invoice attached to the box.
examples.chinese: 发票就附在盒子上。

examples.english: They sent him an invoice at the end of the month.
examples.chinese: 月底他们给他寄来一张发票。

examples.english: We have received an invoice for $250.
examples.chinese: 我们收到一张250美元的发票。

centers.english: to tell someone in writing how much they should pay you
centers.chinese: 用书面方式通知某人该付的金额

examples.english: Some lawyers bill clients up to $300 an hour.
examples.chinese: 有些律师收取高达每小时300美元的服务费。

examples.english: One lobbyist billed the environmental group $20,000 for nine-months’ work.
examples.chinese: 一位说客为这个环保组织工作了九个月，开了一张20,000美元的账单。

examples.english: You will be invoiced as soon as the work is completed.
examples.chinese: 工作一完成就会把发票开给你。

examples.english: The company invoiced us for the cost of using their conference hall.
examples.chinese: 我们使用了该公司的会议厅，他们给我们开了发票。

centers.english: when you are paid or not paid for doing an activity or sport
centers.chinese: 做一件事或参加体育比赛而获得酬劳或不取酬劳

examples.english: Professional basketball players can earn millions of dollars.
examples.chinese: 职业篮球运动员能挣到数百万美元。

examples.english: The play is performed by 50 local actors led by four professionals.
examples.chinese: 这出戏由四名职业演员率领50名当地演员演出。

examples.english: A group of amateur actors performed ‘Romeo and Juliet’.
examples.chinese: 一群业余演员演出了《罗密欧与朱丽叶》。

examples.english: an amateur photographer
examples.chinese: 一位业余摄影师

examples.english: The orchestra is made up entirely of amateurs.
examples.chinese: 这支管弦乐队全部是由业余爱好者组成的。

examples.english: When she retired, she did a lot of voluntary work for the Red Cross.
examples.chinese: 退休后，她为红十字会做了大量义务工作。

examples.english: Perry stayed on with the Agency as an unpaid adviser.
examples.chinese: 佩里作为不计酬的顾问继续留在事务所里工作。

examples.english: Employees were often required to work unpaid overtime.
examples.chinese: 雇员们常常被要求无偿加班。

examples.english: The company allows its employees to take unpaid leave for various reasons.
examples.chinese: 该公司允许其员工请各种不带薪的事假。

centers.english: having a lot of money or possessions

examples.english: Her new boyfriend is very good-looking and very rich.
examples.chinese: 她的新男友很英俊，也很有钱。

examples.english: You have to be rich to afford anything in this shop.
examples.chinese: 你必须很有钱才买得起这家店里的东西。

examples.english: The rich countries of the world have promised more aid for developing countries.
examples.chinese: 世界上的富有国家已答应向发展中国家提供更多的援助。

examples.english: Every year ‘Fortune’ magazine publishes a list of the 100 richest people in America.
examples.chinese: 《财富》杂志每年都公布美国100名最富有者的名单。

examples.english: She comes from a wealthy family, who own houses in London and Paris.
examples.chinese: 她来自一个在伦敦和巴黎都拥有房产的富有家庭。

examples.english: The new taxes were aimed at the largest and wealthiest corporations.
examples.chinese: 新税项是针对规模最大、最富有的公司制定的。

examples.english: You would never have guessed from meeting him how immensely wealthy he was.
examples.chinese: 你看见他的话绝对想不到他有多么富有。

examples.english: The discovery of oil brought great wealth to the area made the people there very wealthy.
examples.chinese: 油田的发现给那个地区带来了巨大的财富。

examples.english: a country where there are extremes of wealth and poverty
examples.chinese: 贫富悬殊的国家

examples.english: They were sufficiently well off to buy their own apartment.
examples.chinese: 他们生活相当富足，能买得起自己的房子。

examples.english: a relatively well off family
examples.chinese: 生活相当富裕的家庭

examples.english: The government claim that people are better off now than they have ever been.
examples.chinese: 政府称现在人民比以前任何时候都富裕。

examples.english: He wants to find a husband from a well-to-do background for his daughter.
examples.chinese: 他想为女儿找一个富家子弟做丈夫。

examples.english: Surprisingly, police statistics show that many of these thefts were carried out by people from well-to-do families.
examples.chinese: 令人吃惊的是，警方的统计显示这些偷窃行为有许多是出身富有的人干的。

examples.english: Educational facilities are best in the more well-to-do residential areas.
examples.chinese: 在更为富裕的住宅区，教育设施条件最好。

examples.english: As people become more affluent, so their standard and style of living improves.
examples.chinese: 人们越来越富裕，他们的生活水准和生活方式也改善了。

examples.english: We drove through affluent suburbs with large houses and tree-lined streets.
examples.chinese: 我们驱车驶过富裕的城郊，那里房舍宽大，街道两边树木林立。

examples.english: Consumer goods are a symbol of prestige in an affluent society.
examples.chinese: 消费品是富裕社会里地位的象征。

examples.english: Since the Second World War there has been an increasing level of affluence in the West.
examples.chinese: 第二次世界大战以后，西方国家越来越富裕。

examples.english: a prosperous American businessman
examples.chinese: 一名富裕的美国商人

examples.english: After the war, Germany became one of Europe's most prosperous countries.
examples.chinese: 战后，德国成了欧洲最繁荣的国家之一。

examples.english: The town's prosperity comes from the textile industry.
examples.chinese: 该镇的繁荣源自纺织工业。

examples.english: When the children were small we never had much money, but now we seem to be comfortably off.
examples.chinese: 孩子们还小的时候我们一直没什么钱，但现在好像宽裕起来了。

examples.english: My wife and I are very lucky, really, we're comfortable, even though we're both retired.
examples.chinese: 我和妻子都很幸运，真的，虽然我们都退休了，但日子过得很宽裕。

examples.english: They've had a much more comfortable life since she started her new job.
examples.chinese: 她开始新的工作以后，他们的生活宽裕多了。

examples.english: He had supposed Mrs Mack to have been in comfortable circumstances, so he was surprised when he saw her tiny apartment.
examples.chinese: 他原以为麦克夫人家境很宽裕，所以看到她那小公寓时吃了一惊。

centers.english: extremely rich

examples.english: Did you know Peter has three houses? He must be loaded.
examples.chinese: 你知道彼得有三幢房子吗？他肯定是极有钱的。

examples.english: Some of her friends are absolutely rolling in it.
examples.chinese: 她的朋友中有些人十分富有。

examples.english: Gregory is filthy rich but he never gives a penny to charity.
examples.chinese: 格雷戈里钱多得发臭，可他对慈善机构从来不捐一分钱。

examples.english: This gorgeous woman walked in, beautifully dressed and obviously stinking rich.
examples.chinese: 这个漂亮女人走了进来，衣着华美，显然富得冒油。

expressions.text: be worth a fortune/be worth millions

examples.english: Michael Jackson must be worth a fortune.
examples.chinese: 迈克尔·杰克逊肯定有万贯家财。

examples.english: The Chief Executive started her career as a secretary, and now she's worth millions.
examples.chinese: 这位总裁以当秘书起家，现在她已拥有百万财产了。

examples.english: Every time I see her she's wearing something new. She must have money to burn.
examples.chinese: 我每次看到她时，她都穿着新衣服。她肯定钱多得花不完了。

examples.english: Unless you've got money to burn, these expensive guitars are not the instruments to get you started.
examples.chinese: 除非你钱多得没地方用，这些昂贵的吉他可不是供初学者用的。

centers.english: having a lot of money now, but not always rich
centers.chinese: 目前有许多钱，但并不总是有钱的

examples.english: I'll buy the drinks, I'm feeling flush just now.
examples.chinese: 我来买酒，我现在正富裕呢。

examples.english: Alan gives his wife fifty dollars a week, or a little more if he's flush
examples.chinese: 艾伦每个星期给妻子50美元，富裕的时候就多给一点。

examples.english: He used to be always wanting a loan, and then all at once, he was in the money.
examples.chinese: 他以前老是要借钱的，后来忽然发财了。

centers.english: a rich person

examples.english: Taylor is a man of means. His family owns several apartment blocks in New York.
examples.chinese: 泰勒是个有钱人，他家在纽约拥有好几幢公寓大楼。

examples.english: These were people of means who could afford to pay expensive legal fees.
examples.chinese: 这些有钱人付得起昂贵的诉讼费。

examples.english: The money to keep the hospital open was provided by a London millionaire.
examples.chinese: 维持这家医院办下去的资金是由伦敦的一位百万富翁提供的。

examples.english: I can't afford a new car just now. I'm no millionaire, you know.
examples.chinese: 我现在买不起新车，要知道我可不是百万富翁。

examples.english: Louis Berg is a multi-millionaire who made his money in the newspaper business.
examples.chinese: 路易斯·伯格是靠报业赚钱的千万富翁。

examples.english: Foundations set up by magnates such as Carnegie and Rockefeller provided most of the funding for the arts in the US.
examples.chinese: 由卡内基和洛克菲勒这类大亨创立的基金会为美国的艺术活动提供了大部分资金。

examples.english: She married a Texan oil magnate.
examples.chinese: 她嫁给了一个得克萨斯的石油大亨。

examples.english: the property magnate who owns the Empire State Building
examples.chinese: 拥有帝国大厦的房地产巨头

examples.english: They do not resent the city fat cats, but believe top businessmen deserve success.
examples.chinese: 他们并不憎恨城里的那些阔佬，而是相信最优秀的商人理应获得成功。

examples.english: Why should those fat cats get rich through our efforts?
examples.chinese: 为什么我们努力劳动却让那些阔佬发财？

examples.english: Nowadays, if you're not a moneybags, some places don't want to have anything to do with you.
examples.chinese: 如今你要不是阔佬的话，有些地方根本就不会理睬你。

examples.english: Under this government the rich seem to have got richer and the poor poorer.
examples.chinese: 在这个政府的领导下，好像富人更富，穷人更穷了。

examples.english: Democracy gave the poor, as well as the rich, a part to play in governing the city.
examples.chinese: 民主让穷人和富人都有机会参与城市的管理。

examples.english: a tour around the Hollywood homes of the rich and famous
examples.chinese: 环游好莱坞富人名流府邸之旅

examples.english: Private health care should not be only for the wealthy.
examples.chinese: 私人医疗保健制度不应该仅仅为富人而设。

examples.english: The hotel stood by a lake, where the wealthy went to go fishing or pigeon shooting.
examples.chinese: 酒店坐落在湖边，有钱人上那儿钓鱼、打泥鸽。

expressions.text: the haves and the have nots

examples.english: The widening gap between the haves and the have nots is becoming very noticeable in New York.
examples.chinese: 贫富差距越来越大，这在纽约已经非常明显了。

centers.english: to become rich

examples.english: I'm trying to think of a way to get rich.
examples.chinese: 我在设法想出一条致富之路。

examples.english: Mr Askin got rich selling second hand cars.
examples.chinese: 阿斯金先生靠出售二手车致富。

examples.english: The company promised its sales team that, if they worked hard enough, they would get rich quick.
examples.chinese: 公司向营销人员保证，如果他们干得卖力，很快就能发财。

expressions.text: make a fortune/bundle/ make a bomb

examples.english: If you're a good salesman you can make a bomb, but you have to work hard.
examples.chinese: 如果你是位出色的销售员，你就能发大财，但必须努力工作。

examples.english: You shouldn't have sold your shares. You could have made a bundle.
examples.chinese: 你不该把股票卖掉，本来你可以赚大钱的。

expressions.text: make a fortune etc (by) doing something

examples.english: Richard made a fortune breeding racehorses.
examples.chinese: 理查德靠养赛马发了大财。

examples.english: He's just a poor country boy who made good in the city.
examples.chinese: 他只是一个在城里发家的乡下穷小子。

examples.english: Ian thinks that just because he made good, everybody else can too.
examples.chinese: 伊恩认为既然他发家了，其他人也可以。

examples.english: If you don't have any yourself, it's a pretty smart move to marry into money!
examples.chinese: 假如你本身一无所有，那么跟有钱人结婚倒不失为聪明的做法！

examples.english: Although he came from a poor family, he managed to go from rags to riches.
examples.chinese: 虽然他出身贫寒，可是终于发迹了。

examples.english: a classic rags-to-riches story
examples.chinese: 一则典型的白手起家的故事

examples.english: Now he's got a contract to advertise sports clothing he'll be made for life.
examples.chinese: 他已签约为运动装做广告，这下他一辈子就不愁钱用了。

examples.english: Sylvester Stallone need never make another movie. He's already set up for life.
examples.chinese: 西尔维斯特·史泰龙不需要再拍电影了，他的钱已经够他享用一辈子了。

centers.english: ways of telling someone that you are not rich
centers.chinese: 告诉人们你不富有的说法

examples.english: ‘Why don't you move to a bigger house?’ ‘I'm not made of money, you know!’
examples.chinese: “你为什么不搬到一个大一点的房子去住？”“要知道我并不很富有啊！”

examples.english: Look how much you've spent on clothes this month! Money doesn't grow on trees, you know!
examples.chinese: 看看你这个月花了多少钱买衣服！你要知道钱可不是树上长出来的！

centers.english: when you wish that something had not happened or was not true
centers.chinese: 希望某事没有发生过或不是真的

examples.english: There's nothing I can do about it, unfortunately.
examples.chinese: 很遗憾，我对此无能为力。

examples.english: Unfortunately, Dr Cole cannot spend as long with each patient as she would like.
examples.chinese: 令人遗憾的是，科尔医生不能按照她自己的意愿在每个病人身上都多花时间。

examples.english: We took some fantastic photos, but unfortunately the film got damaged.
examples.chinese: 我们拍了一些很棒的照片，可惜底片坏了。

examples.english: Sadly, this fine old theatre was destroyed by fire in 1993.
examples.chinese: 不幸的是，这座漂亮的旧剧院在1993年被烧毁了。

examples.english: Alice was rushed to hospital, but sadly she died two hours later.
examples.chinese: 艾丽斯被火速地送到了医院，但不幸的是，两小时以后她死了。

examples.english: It's a pity about the weather - it was so nice yesterday.
examples.chinese: 天气不好真是可惜—昨天还好好的。

examples.english: It's a shame you can't come with us.
examples.chinese: 真遗憾你不能和我们一起去。

examples.english: ‘Janet didn't get that job.’ ‘Oh, what a pity!’
examples.chinese: “珍妮特没有得到那份工作。”“啊，真可惜！”

examples.english: They've cut down all those beautiful trees. It's a terrible shame.
examples.chinese: 他们把那些漂亮的树都砍光了，太不像话了。

examples.english: It's so sad that your father can't be here to see this.
examples.chinese: 你爸爸不能来这里看到这一切，真令人难过。

examples.english: It's sad when a marriage breaks up, especially after all those years.
examples.chinese: 婚姻破裂让人心酸，尤其是经历了那么多年的婚姻。

examples.english: The town centre is dying, and most of the shops have closed down. It's terribly sad.
examples.chinese: 城镇中心在败落，大部分商铺都关门了。真令人悲哀。

examples.english: ‘Senator Volk's out of town.’ ‘Too bad! I wanted to meet him and talk about the campaign.’
examples.chinese: “沃克参议员已经出城了。”“真不巧！我还想跟他讨论一下有关竞选的事情呢。”

examples.english: Too bad Dickie isn't here to enjoy the fun.
examples.chinese: 迪基不在这里享受这愉快的时刻，真可惜。

examples.english: It's too bad you have to leave, just when we need you most.
examples.chinese: 真是太糟糕了，就在我们最需要你的时候你却要走了。

examples.english: The wedding was lovely. It was just unfortunate about the rain.
examples.chinese: 婚礼很有意思，只是下雨有些遗憾。

examples.english: It's unfortunate that you have to travel so far to work.
examples.chinese: 很不幸你要走这么远的路去上班。

examples.english: Now I'm too old to fall in love, more's the pity.
examples.chinese: 我现在已超过了谈恋爱的年龄，太可惜了。

examples.english: I'm afraid this car doesn't belong to me, more's the pity.
examples.chinese: 恐怕这辆车不是我的，很遗憾啊。

examples.english: Regrettably Jousse's work has not been translated into English.
examples.chinese: 令人遗憾的是茹斯的作品未被翻译成英语。

examples.english: The poor and disadvantaged will, regrettably, be the ones to suffer as a result of the new law.
examples.chinese: 遗憾的是，穷人和弱势群体将成为新法律的牺牲品。

centers.english: something that you wish had not happened or was not true
centers.chinese: 希望某事没有发生过或不是真实的

examples.english: Parents are so busy with their careers that they don't have time to have fun with their children, and that's unfortunate.
examples.chinese: 父母忙于事业而没有时间陪孩子玩，真可惜。

examples.english: ‘It was an unfortunate set of circumstances that no one could have predicted,’ a spokesperson said today.
examples.chinese: “这些令人遗憾的情况没有人能预料得到。”今天一位发言人说。

examples.english: ‘This is an unfortunate and extremely regrettable incident,’ the minister told a newspaper.
examples.chinese: “这是一起令人极其遗憾的不幸事故。”部长对一家报社说道。

examples.english: It was decided by the authorities that the building of the dam across the valley was a ‘regrettable necessity’.
examples.chinese: 当局认为，修建横跨山谷的大坝是“令人遗憾的不得已之举”。

centers.english: short in length or distance

examples.english: These curtains are much too short.
examples.chinese: 这些窗帘太短了。

examples.english: She has short curly hair and wears glasses.
examples.chinese: 她有短短的鬈发，戴着眼镜。

examples.english: a short-sleeved T-shirt
examples.chinese: 一件短袖T恤|You look different your hair's shorter.你看起来不一样了一你的头发剪短了。

examples.english: The hotel is just a short distance from the station.
examples.chinese: 宾馆离车站只有很短的路程。

examples.english: Chris went for a short walk to clear his head.
examples.chinese: 克里斯出去走了走，清醒清醒头脑。

examples.english: Sandy took a short cut home.
examples.chinese: 桑迪抄了一条近路回家。

examples.english: Do you know any short cuts to the hospital?
examples.chinese: 你知道去医院有近路可以走吗？

examples.english: Pheasants have short stubby wings which enable them to fly very fast and low.
examples.chinese: 野鸡的翅膀又粗又短，所以它们能飞得很快很低。

examples.english: a fat little boy with stumpy legs
examples.chinese: 两腿粗短的胖小子

centers.english: when something you say or write is short
centers.chinese: 说的话或写的东西简短的

examples.english: Graham made a short speech of thanks after the ceremony.
examples.chinese: 仪式结束后，格雷厄姆致简短谢辞。

examples.english: a book of short stories
examples.chinese: 短篇小说集

examples.english: The chapters are really short, so I read a couple every night.
examples.chinese: 章节很短，所以我每天晚上读几章。

examples.english: We had a short pep talk from the coach before the game.
examples.chinese: 比赛开始前，教练短短地讲了几句鼓舞士气的话。

examples.english: Please write a short paragraph explaining your reasons for applying to this college.
examples.chinese: 请写一段简短的文字，说明你申请就读本校的原因。

examples.english: The book begins with a brief outline of the history of modern China.
examples.chinese: 本书开头部分简单介绍了中国现代历史。

examples.english: We just have to write a very brief piece on what we did in the vacation.
examples.chinese: 我们只要写一篇很短的文字，说说假期里我们干了些什么。

examples.english: There was a brief note with the flowers.
examples.chinese: 送来的花附了一张小纸条。

examples.english: Saussure expressed his arguments in a concise and logical way.
examples.chinese: 索绪尔把论点阐述得简洁明了，而且很有条理。

examples.english: Sergeant Hanks gave us concise, sensible instructions.
examples.chinese: 汉克斯中士给我们下达了简洁而合理的命令。

examples.english: Make sure that your answers are as clear and concise as possible.
examples.chinese: 回答一定要尽量简洁清晰。

examples.english: The new labelling is more succinct and advises consumers simply that oat bran may help prevent heart disease.
examples.chinese: 新的标签更加简洁扼要，只忠告消费者说燕麦麸有助于预防心脏病。

examples.english: Spell out your work objectives clearly and succinctly.
examples.chinese: 清楚扼要地说一说你的工作目标。

examples.english: As Susan put it so succinctly ‘No overtime pay, no work!’
examples.chinese: 苏珊已经说得很清楚了，“没有加班费，不加班！”

centers.english: to make something shorter

examples.english: I heard she had an operation to shorten her nose.
examples.chinese: 我听说她动手术把鼻子做短了。

examples.english: You can improve your writing just by shortening some of these long sentences.
examples.chinese: 只要把这些长句子改短，你的文章就可以好一些。

examples.english: It costs £12 to get trousers shortened.
examples.chinese: 裤子改短要12英镑。

examples.english: This essay's still too long, I'll have to shorten it by a couple of thousand words.
examples.chinese: 这篇论文还是太长，我得把它缩短几千字。

examples.english: His name's Lawrence, but it's usually shortened to Larry.
examples.chinese: 他的名字是劳伦斯，但是一般简称拉里。

examples.english: You could make your speech shorter by taking out all the quotations.
examples.chinese: 你可以删掉所有引文，把演说稿改短一些。

expressions.text: make something one metre/two centimetres etc shorter

examples.english: Engineers have removed a section of the pipeline, making it about a hundred metres shorter.
examples.chinese: 工程师拿掉了一段管道，把它缩短了约一百米。

examples.english: Even after it had been cut, the film was still over three hours long.
examples.chinese: 这部电影即使删剪过之后仍长达三个多小时。

examples.english: I had to cut huge chunks out to get this essay to the right length.
examples.chinese: 我得把这篇论文大段大段地删节至适当的篇幅。

examples.english: It's so difficult to cut even a couple of scenes from a play without losing some of the story.
examples.chinese: 一个剧本即使只是删一两个场面，也很难做到不丢失故事情节的。

examples.english: The introduction's too long. Can you try and cut it down?
examples.chinese: 引言太长了，你能否试试作一些删略?

examples.english: Did you have to cut your dissertation down?
examples.chinese: 你的论文必须要缩短吗?

examples.english: They want me to cut down my article so that it'll all fit onto one page.
examples.chinese: 他们要我把文章作一些删节，这样就能排在一页里面。

examples.english: I'd like to condense that statement still further.
examples.chinese: 我想把那个声明再浓缩一下。

examples.english: Hawkins condensed all his writings into one volume for publication.
examples.chinese: 霍金斯把他所有的文章都精简成一册来出版。

examples.english: How could he condense all he had lived through into a sixty-minute speech?
examples.chinese: 他是如何把他所经历的一切浓缩成一场60分钟的演说的？

centers.english: when a book or piece of writing has been made shorter

examples.english: This chapter is a shortened version of a paper that was written in 1977.
examples.chinese: 这一章是1977年写的一篇论文的删节版本。

examples.english: a shortened version of the Jewish creed
examples.chinese: 犹太教义的缩略本

examples.english: The book contains many of the most popular stories from the Bible in shortened form.
examples.chinese: 这本书里有许多经过缩略的最广为流传的圣经故事。

examples.english: The following article is an abridged version of a speech given by Porter in May 2000.
examples.chinese: 下面的文章是一篇波特在2000年5月发表的一次演讲的删节本。

examples.english: The book is an abridged account of his experiences in India before Independence.
examples.chinese: 这本书是一个删节本，叙述了印度独立前他在那里的经历。

centers.english: to say or write something using as few words as possible
centers.chinese: 用尽量少的语言说或写

examples.english: Lieutenant, I'll be brief and I'll be candid -- when do you plan to leave?
examples.chinese: 中尉，我就长话短说，单刀直入了—你打算什么时候走?

examples.english: I'm sure you're all very busy, so I'll be brief.
examples.chinese: 我知道你们都很忙，所以我长话短说。

examples.english: I'll be as brief as possible so as not to waste your time.
examples.chinese: 我尽量说得简短一些，不浪费你们的时间。

examples.english: I'll keep it short as I don't have much time.
examples.chinese: 我时间不多，所以就长话短说了。

examples.english: Tell me, but keep it short, I'm in the middle of something.
examples.chinese: 说吧，不过要简短些，我正有事儿。

expressions.text: keep it short and sweet/short and simple

examples.english: Mr Chairman, I think I've got five minutes, so I'll keep it fairly short and sweet.
examples.chinese: 主席先生，我想我有五分钟时间，所以我会讲得十分简短扼要。

centers.english: when a name or word is a shorter way of saying something
centers.chinese: 名字或单词是某事物的缩略方式

examples.english: VHF is short for Very High Frequency.
examples.chinese: VHF是Very High Frequency〔甚高频〕的缩写。

examples.english: ‘Is ‘Shelley’ short for anything?’ ‘Yes, my real name's Michelle.’
examples.chinese: “Shelley是简称吧？”“是的，我的全名是Michelle。”

examples.english: What's ‘ISP’ short for?
examples.chinese: ISP是什么的缩写？

examples.english: ‘What does ‘NAC’ stand for?’ ‘National Aerobics Championships’.
examples.chinese: “NAC指的是什么？”“

examples.english: On a US ship, you see ‘USS’, standing for ‘United States Ship’.
examples.chinese: 美国的船只上写有USS字样，代表United States Ship〔美国船〕。

examples.english: The ‘F’ in ‘John F Kennedy’ stood for ‘Fitzgerald’.
examples.chinese: 约翰·F.肯尼迪中的F代表Fitzgerald〔菲茨杰拉德〕。

examples.english: Hi, my name's Moses -- Mo for short.
examples.chinese: 嗨，我叫摩西—简称摩。

examples.english: He's actually called Jeremy, but everyone who knows him calls him Jem for short.
examples.chinese: 他其实叫杰里米，但认识他的人都叫他杰姆，那是他的简称。

examples.english: Is it correct to abbreviate ‘Avenue’, ‘Street’ and so on when writing an address on an envelope?
examples.chinese: 在信封上写地址的时候，Avenue、Street等词用缩写正确吗?

examples.english: The word ‘kilogram' is usually abbreviated to ’kg'.
examples.chinese: 单词kilogram〔千克〕通常缩写成kg。

examples.english: Disk Operating Systems are usually known by the abbreviation DOS.
examples.chinese: 磁盘操作系统通常简称DOS。I

examples.english: a Dictionary of Acronyms and Abbreviations
examples.chinese: 一本缩略语辞典

examples.english: I never knew the abbreviation ‘GI’ stood for ‘Government Issue’.
examples.chinese: 我从来都不知道GI是Government Issue〔政府发放〕的缩写。

examples.english: BBC is an abbreviation for British Broadcasting Corporation.
examples.chinese: BBC是British Broadcasting Corporation〔英国广播公司〕的缩写。

centers.english: not tall

examples.english: ‘What does she look like?’ ‘She's short and fat, with brown hair.’
examples.chinese: “她长什么样子？”“她又矮又胖，头发是棕色的。”

examples.english: a short, stocky man with powerful shoulders
examples.chinese: 一个肩膀强壮有力、矮小敦实的男子

examples.english: Mr Haddad was several inches shorter than his wife.
examples.chinese: 哈达德先生比他太太矮几英寸。

examples.english: She's not very tall - about 1.4 metres, I'd say.
examples.chinese: 她个子不是很高—我想大概一米四左右。

examples.english: Well, I'm not very tall and my legs are short, so I always had trouble in the hurdle race.
examples.chinese: 嗯，我个子不高，腿又短，所以参加跨栏比赛总是很麻烦。

examples.english: a small man in a dark suit
examples.chinese: 身穿一套黑衣服的矮个子男人

examples.english: How come I always seem to go out with small men?
examples.chinese: 我怎么好像总是和矮个子的男人约会？

examples.english: My sister's quite a bit smaller and slimmer than me.
examples.chinese: 我妹妹比我矮小一些，苗条一些。

examples.english: Bobby's small for his age, but he's perfectly healthy.
examples.chinese: 以博比的年龄来说，他算是矮小的，但是他身体非常健康。

examples.english: We saw a little old lady with a walking-stick.
examples.chinese: 我们看到一位身材矮小、拄着拐杖的老太太。

examples.english: Who's this little boy in the blue sweater?
examples.chinese: 穿蓝毛衣的那个小男孩是谁？

examples.english: I haven't seen one of those since I was a little girl.
examples.chinese: 从我还是个小女孩的时候起，就再也没见过那东西了。

examples.english: His wife was a petite dark-haired woman in her early thirties.
examples.chinese: 他妻子娇小玲珑，黑色头发，三十刚出头。

examples.english: Brandon's quite stocky really, isn't he?
examples.chinese: 布兰登长得很敦实，是不是？

examples.english: He's a big stocky bloke and he plays rugby.
examples.chinese: 他是个大块头，长得很敦实，喜欢打橄榄球。

examples.english: The cook was short and squat, with thick eyebrows and a slight moustache.
examples.chinese: 厨师又矮又胖，眉毛粗黑，留了一撮稀疏的小胡子。

examples.english: a shabby, squat, balding man in an old raincoat
examples.chinese: 一个穿着旧雨衣、衣衫褴褛、矮矮胖胖、有些秃顶的男人

centers.english: very short

examples.english: A tiny old lady answered the door.
examples.chinese: 一位个子非常矮小的老太太开了门。

examples.english: She's tiny, but she belts out these old blues songs like you wouldn't believe.
examples.chinese: 她长得非常矮小，但是她放声高唱起这些老的蓝调歌曲的样子，你是难以相信的。

examples.english: They look so funny together. She's really tiny and her husband's about six foot five.
examples.chinese: 他们俩在一起看上去很滑稽，她很矮小，而她丈夫大概有6英尺5英寸左右。

examples.english: She was holding a tiny little baby in her arms.
examples.chinese: 她怀里抱着一个丁点大的婴儿。

examples.english: Peter was a shy, diminutive man who seldom said anything to anyone.
examples.chinese: 彼得是个腼腆、瘦小的人，很少跟人说话。

examples.english: A diminutive figure appeared in the doorway.
examples.chinese: 一个瘦小的身影出现在门口。

centers.english: a short time

examples.english: Unfortunately, we could only spend a short time together.
examples.chinese: 遗憾的是，我们能在一起的时间只有很短一段。

examples.english: The talk should only last a short time.
examples.chinese: 会谈应该只持续很短的一段时间。

examples.english: How did you manage to do all this in such a short time?
examples.chinese: 你是怎样做到在这么短的时间内干完这些事情的?

examples.english: The police arrived within a very short time.
examples.chinese: 警察没一会儿就赶到了。

examples.english: Your friends left a short time ago.
examples.chinese: 你朋友走了没多久。

examples.english: I think he went to prison for a short time.
examples.chinese: 我想他坐牢坐了很短时间。

examples.english: It always takes a little while to get used to the climate.
examples.chinese: 适应气候总需要一段时间。

examples.english: Bob's only worked here for a short while, about six months I think.
examples.chinese: 鲍勃在这儿只工作了很短的一段时间，我想大概六个月吧。

expressions.text: a little while/a short while ago

examples.english: He died a little while ago.
examples.chinese: 他不久前过世了。

examples.english: She was in the papers a short while ago.
examples.chinese: 她不久前上过报纸。

examples.english: If you take the pills now, your headache will go after a short while.
examples.chinese: 如果你现在把药吃了，头痛一会儿就能好。

examples.english: Don't start that now, it'll be time to go in a little while.
examples.chinese: 不要现在开始做那事，过一会儿就要走了。

examples.english: Just a moment Susie, can I have a quick word with you?
examples.chinese: 等一会儿，苏茜，我和你说一两句话好吗?

examples.english: Can I borrow your pen a minute?
examples.chinese: 借你的笔用一下可以吗？

examples.english: Wait a minute, I'm nearly ready.
examples.chinese: 等一会儿，我快准备好了。

examples.english: Where's Charles gone? He was here a moment ago.
examples.chinese: 查尔斯去哪儿了？他刚才还在这里。

examples.english: Sit down for a minute and rest your legs.
examples.chinese: 坐一会儿，让你的腿歇歇。

examples.english: Mark should be back in a moment.
examples.chinese: 马克应该一会儿就回来。

examples.english: I'll be with you in a minute.
examples.chinese: 我一会儿就回来陪你。

examples.english: Do you mind switching the telly on a second?
examples.chinese: 开一会儿电视行吗？

examples.english: An instant later, she let out a piercing scream.
examples.chinese: 没过多久，她发出了一声尖叫。

examples.english: ‘Yes,’ she declared, without an instant's hesitation.
examples.chinese: “是的。”她毫不犹豫地宣称。

examples.english: ‘Have you finished writing?’ ‘No, hang on a second.’
examples.chinese: “你写完了吗？”“没有，等一会儿。”

examples.english: Can I stop you there, just for a second?
examples.chinese: 打扰你一下行吗？就一会儿。

examples.english: Just hold that end for a second while I fix this to the wall.
examples.chinese: 你拿着那头一会儿，我把这头钉到墙上。

examples.english: Did her eyes flicker open for an instant?
examples.chinese: 她的眼睛有没有眨开过一会儿？

examples.english: Mr Smart's on the other line, can he call you back in a second?
examples.chinese: 斯马特先生在听另一个电话，叫他过一会儿打给你好吗?

examples.english: We both fell asleep in an instant.
examples.chinese: 我们俩都是一下子就睡着了。

examples.english: I waited, and a bit later the phone went again -- it was Bill.
examples.chinese: 我等了一会儿，电话铃又响了—是比尔。

examples.english: Oh, wait a bit, can't you?
examples.chinese: 噢，等等行吗？

examples.english: I think I'll lie down for a bit.
examples.chinese: 我想我要躺一会儿。

examples.english: ‘Are you coming?’ ‘Yes, in a bit.’
examples.chinese: “你来吗？”“来，马上来。”

examples.english: After a bit, Bill had started to tire of her company.
examples.chinese: 没过一会儿，比尔就厌倦和她在一起了。

centers.english: continuing for only a short time

examples.english: The meeting was shorter than I'd expected.
examples.chinese: 开会时间比我原来预计的要短。

examples.english: the shortest day of the year
examples.chinese: 一年中最短的一天

examples.english: a short course in aromatherapy
examples.chinese: 香薰疗法短期课程

examples.english: It would have been better if they'd closed the road for a short period of time while the repairs were done.
examples.chinese: 修路的时候，他们还是封路一段时间较好。

examples.english: I took a quick look at the map.
examples.chinese: 我很快查了一下地图。

examples.english: Do I have time for a quick shower before we go out?
examples.chinese: 我们出去之前，我赶快洗个淋浴来得及吗？

examples.english: She's going to give me a quick lesson on Feng Shui this afternoon.
examples.chinese: 她今天下午要很快地给我上一堂风水课。

examples.english: Can I ask just one quick question?
examples.chinese: 我就问一个小问题，行吗？

examples.english: It was impossible to see everything during our brief visit to Paris.
examples.chinese: 我们在巴黎停留时间很短，不可能什么都看。

examples.english: After a brief intermission, the performance continued.
examples.chinese: 短短的幕间休息过后，演出继续进行。

examples.english: Let me show you how to use the program -- it won't take long.
examples.chinese: 我来教你怎么用这个程序—花不了多少时间。

examples.english: We'll have the chicken drumsticks - they won't take long to thaw.
examples.chinese: 我们就吃鸡腿吧—鸡腿解冻快。

expressions.text: it doesn't take (somebody) long to do something

examples.english: It didn't take long to solve the problem.
examples.chinese: 没多久问题就解决了。

examples.english: It sure didn't take you long to smell the food!
examples.chinese: 有好吃的东西当然一下子就闻出来了！

examples.english: The doctor says the swelling is just temporary and should go down in a few days.
examples.chinese: 医生说发肿只是暂时的，过几天就会消下去。

examples.english: a temporary driver's license
examples.chinese: 临时驾驶执照

examples.english: They're living in temporary accommodation at the moment.
examples.chinese: 他们目前住在一个临时居所里。

examples.english: Demand for temporary workers continues to rise.
examples.chinese: 对临时工的需求在继续增长。

examples.english: Ben's found a temporary job until November.
examples.chinese: 本找到了一份做到11月份的临时工作。

examples.english: The library is temporarily closed for repairs.
examples.chinese: 图书馆因为整修暂时关闭。

examples.english: They had a passionate but short-lived affair.
examples.chinese: 他们之间有过一段充满激情但是短暂的关系。

examples.english: We were glad to be home, but our happiness was short-lived.
examples.chinese: 我们很高兴回到了家里，但是我们的幸福并不长久。

examples.english: However, the President's popularity may prove to be short-lived.
examples.chinese: 然而，总统的支持率也许难以长久。

examples.english: At the time, I didn't give Alison so much as a passing thought -- I had other things on my mind.
examples.chinese: 当时我没有想到艾莉森，连闪过一个念头都没有—我脑子里在想其他事情。

examples.english: Most people take only a passing interest in their horoscope.
examples.chinese: 大多数人对于自己的星象都只有一时的兴趣。

examples.english: Whether this is just a passing fad or a lasting fashion trend, only time will tell.
examples.chinese: 这只是一时的风尚还是持久的潮流，唯有时间才能证明。

examples.english: No dictionary can really capture something as fleeting and ephemeral as slang.
examples.chinese: 没有一本辞典能真正地把昙花一现、转瞬即逝的俚语记录下来。

examples.english: Hopes of political unity in the region have proved ephemeral.
examples.chinese: 在该地区实现政治上的统一，这一希望证明只是昙花一现。

centers.english: tight clothes

examples.english: This skirt is far too tight.
examples.chinese: 这条裙子太紧了。

examples.english: Tight shoes can cause corns and other foot problems.
examples.chinese: 太紧的鞋子会引发鸡眼以及其他足病。

examples.english: I never wear tight clothes - I just don't feel comfortable in them.
examples.chinese: 我从不穿紧身衣服，穿了会觉得不舒服。

examples.english: Suzy was wearing a sleeveless skintight dress and sandals.
examples.chinese: 苏西穿了件无袖紧身连衣裙和一双凉鞋。

examples.english: a pair of skintight jeans
examples.chinese: 一条紧身牛仔裤

examples.english: Standing next to him was a small, thin woman in a close-fitting black suit.
examples.chinese: 站在他身边的是一个穿紧身黑色西服的瘦小女人。

examples.english: The diver's wet suit should be tight-fitting, yet allow reasonable freedom of movement.
examples.chinese: 潜水员穿的潜水服应该是紧身的，但又能让身体有一定的活动自由。

centers.english: when a rope, wire etc has been pulled or stretched tight
centers.chinese: 绳、线等被拉紧或绷紧的说法

examples.english: If the straps aren't tight enough, the saddle can slip.
examples.chinese: 如果带子不够紧，马鞍会滑下来。

examples.english: ‘Seat belt tight? Good, this could be a rough ride.’
examples.chinese: “安全带系紧了吗？好的，这段路程可能很崎岖。”

examples.english: Sylvia handed me a large parcel, tightly wrapped in brown paper.
examples.chinese: 西尔维娅递给我一个用棕色纸紧紧包住的大包裹。

examples.english: The skin of his face felt dry and taut.
examples.chinese: 感觉他脸上的皮肤又干又紧。

examples.english: He massaged the taut muscles of her neck.
examples.chinese: 他给她绷紧的颈部肌肉做按摩。

examples.english: A single strand of taut barbed wire was strung along the top of the garden wall.
examples.chinese: 一条绷紧的带刺铁丝拉在花园围墙上。

centers.english: fastened tight

examples.english: Check that the screws are tight.
examples.chinese: 检查一下螺丝钉是否拧紧了。

examples.english: Cover with a tight lid and refrigerate.
examples.chinese: 用密封盖子盖好后冷藏。

examples.english: The posts must be fixed firmly in the ground.
examples.chinese: 柱子必须牢牢固定在地上。

examples.english: When leaving the house, check that all doors and windows are firmly closed and locked.
examples.chinese: 离开屋子之前要检查一下门窗是否都关紧锁好。

examples.english: We made sure that our bags were securely fastened to the roof of the car.
examples.chinese: 我们确保已经把旅行袋都牢靠地系在车顶上。

examples.english: A large safety screen, securely fixed to the wall, will prevent a child coming into contact with a fire.
examples.chinese: 一块很大的安全隔板牢固地固定在墙上，可以防止儿童接触到火。

examples.english: The front of the boat was stuck fast in the mud.
examples.chinese: 船头深深地陷在泥中。

examples.english: The spare tyre on the back of the Jeep was held fast by three strong bolts.
examples.chinese: 吉普车后面的备用轮胎被三个坚固的螺栓牢牢固定住。

centers.english: to make something tight

examples.english: Tighten the screws gradually until the wheel is firmly in place.
examples.chinese: 慢慢地拧紧螺丝直到轮子固定好。

examples.english: He changed the spanner for one the correct size and tightened the nut.
examples.chinese: 他换了把尺寸合适的扳手把螺帽拧紧。

examples.english: I think the fan belt needs to be tightened.
examples.chinese: 我觉得风扇皮带需要拉紧一点。

examples.english: Brian wrapped some string round the parcel and pulled it tight.
examples.chinese: 布赖恩把一些细绳套在包裹外，然后抽紧。

examples.english: She buttoned her jacket all the way up and pulled the collar tight around her neck.
examples.chinese: 她把外套上的纽扣由下往上全扣好，并把衣领拉紧围住脖子。

examples.english: Seth stretched the phone cord around the corner so that he could speak in private.
examples.chinese: 塞思把电话线拉到转角处，这样就可以不让人听见自己说的话。

examples.english: He stretched a large tarpaulin over the vehicle, tying it down at the corners.
examples.chinese: 他把一块很大的防水帆布拉开盖在车上，把几个角系牢。

centers.english: to sell something

examples.english: Tom's thinking of selling his motorcycle and buying a new one.
examples.chinese: 汤姆在考虑把摩托车卖了，再买一辆新的。

examples.english: If you can, wait to sell until prices are high.
examples.chinese: 你如果能等的话，就等到价格高了再卖。

examples.english: Postcards and souvenirs were being sold outside the cathedral.
examples.chinese: 教堂外面有明信片和纪念品出售。

examples.english: The company sold Braugh $100,000 worth of computers at discounted rates.
examples.chinese: 公司以优惠价向布劳夫出售了价值10万美元的电脑。

examples.english: It is illegal to sell tobacco to anyone under the age of 18.
examples.chinese: 向18岁以下者出售烟草是非法的。

examples.english: The painting was sold to an art gallery in Philadelphia.
examples.chinese: 这幅画卖给了费城的一家画廊。

examples.english: The antique buttons are very valuable, and we sell them for £100 and upwards.
examples.chinese: 这些古董纽扣非常值钱，我们卖一百英镑或以上。

examples.english: The bakery usually sells its cakes off at half price just before closing time.
examples.chinese: 面包店通常在关门前半价出售糕点。

examples.english: After the funeral the house contents were sold off quickly to pay all his debts.
examples.chinese: 葬礼后，屋里的东西被迅速变卖来偿还他的债务。

examples.english: The school district may be forced to close schools and sell off land in order to make much-needed repairs.
examples.chinese: 这个学区可能要被迫关闭学校、变卖土地，以便进行急需的修缮工作。

examples.english: Japanese televisions and hi-fi systems are exported all over the world.
examples.chinese: 日本的电视机以及高保真音响设备出口到世界各地。

examples.english: Ancient artefacts cannot be exported.
examples.chinese: 古代的手工艺品不得出口。

expressions.text: export something to France/the UK/Japan etc

examples.english: In 1986 they exported 210,000 cases of wine to the UK.
examples.chinese: 1986年他们向英国出口了21万箱葡萄酒。

examples.english: Saudi Arabia is one of the world's leading exporters of oil.
examples.chinese: 沙特阿拉伯是世界上主要的石油出口国之一。

examples.english: Export earnings are lower than last year.
examples.chinese: 出口收益比去年低。

examples.english: Why don't we sell up and move to Canada? Property is really cheap there!
examples.chinese: 我们为什么不卖掉房子移居加拿大呢？那儿的地产很便宜！

examples.english: My parents sold up the farm and went to live in Glasgow.
examples.chinese: 我父母卖掉农场，住到了格拉斯哥。

examples.english: There was a line of people in front of me who were all waiting to be served.
examples.chinese: 我前面排了一队人，都等着有人来服务。

examples.english: Estrada was serving a customer when the fire started.
examples.chinese: 起火时埃斯特拉达正在接待一位顾客。

examples.english: It is illegal to serve alcoholic drinks to anyone under 18.
examples.chinese: 向18岁以下的人出售酒精饮料是非法的。

examples.english: The gallery deals mostly in paintings but they do sometimes sell photographs.
examples.chinese: 这家画廊主要经营画作，但有时也卖一些摄影作品。

examples.english: Tax agents are visiting more than 5,000 businesses that deal in expensive items such as cars, boats, and jewels.
examples.chinese: 税务员查访了五千多家公司，这些公司都经营汽车、船只和珠宝等高价商品。

examples.english: The rebels are using money from the sale of drugs to buy weapons.
examples.chinese: 叛军在利用贩毒所得的钱购买武器。

examples.english: The law prohibits the sale and consumption of alcohol on unlicensed premises.
examples.chinese: 法律禁止在没有经营许可证的场地出售和饮用酒类。

examples.english: There was a man at the market who was flogging watches for £10 each.
examples.chinese: 市场上有个人在出售手表，每块10英镑。

examples.english: Don't let him flog you his car -- he's had endless trouble with it.
examples.chinese: 别让他把汽车卖给你—那辆车毛病不断。

examples.english: Street vendors peddled flowers and candles.
examples.chinese: 街头小贩兜售鲜花和蜡烛。

examples.english: Belloni started her bakery business by peddling her homemade bread to local stores.
examples.chinese: 贝洛妮的面包店生意是从她向当地商店推销她自制的面包做起的。

examples.english: Trading started briskly on the New York Stock Exchange this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天早上纽约证券交易所开盘时交投活跃。

examples.english: The company ran out of money and was forced to cease trading.
examples.chinese: 公司没有钱了，只得把生意停了。

centers.english: to sell illegal goods

examples.english: The gang earned as much as $10 million a month peddling heroin and cocaine.
examples.chinese: 这个黑帮贩卖海洛因和可卡因，一个月有多达一千万美元的收入。

examples.english: Stricter punishments will be given to those convicted of peddling drugs to children.
examples.chinese: 对于向儿童出售毒品的罪犯，将会加重量刑。

examples.english: The police suspect him of dealing in stolen goods.
examples.chinese: 警方怀疑他买卖赃物。

examples.english: Police believe Fry was dealing in narcotics.
examples.chinese: 警方认为弗莱伊在从事毒品交易。

examples.english: The article accuses Davis of dealing in arms.
examples.chinese: 文章指控戴维斯在从事军火交易。

examples.english: Anyone caught pushing heroin or cocaine is given a long prison sentence.
examples.chinese: 凡因贩卖海洛因或可卡因被捕的人，都要判长期徒刑。

examples.english: In Thailand the penalty for drug trafficking is death.
examples.chinese: 在泰国，毒品走私要判死刑。

examples.english: Customs officials claim to have uncovered a major drug-trafficking ring.
examples.chinese: 海关官员称已破获一个很大的毒品走私团伙。

examples.english: The two girls were arrested for trafficking in hard drugs.
examples.chinese: 这两个女孩因走私硬毒品被捕。

examples.english: Authorities are worried about the growing black market in the city.
examples.chinese: 当局担心市内黑市规模在扩大。

examples.english: You can get a much better rate for your dollar on the black market than in a bank.
examples.chinese: 黑市上的美元汇率比银行的高多了。

examples.english: The immigration service is concerned about a thriving black market in phoney ID cards and working permits.
examples.chinese: 移民局担心黑市伪造身份证和工作许可证的生意越来越畅旺。

examples.english: A man came up to me in the street and offered to sell me some black market cigarettes.
examples.chinese: 街上有个男人走上来向我兜售黑市香烟。

examples.english: The people here are forced to pay exorbitant black market prices for everyday goods such as food and clothes.
examples.chinese: 这里的人被迫要出高得过分的黑市价购买食物、衣服等日用品。

centers.english: to sell something to the person who offers the most money
centers.chinese: 把某物售予出价最高的人

examples.english: One of the Beatles’ guitars is being auctioned for charity.
examples.chinese: 披头士乐队的一把吉他正在进行慈善拍卖。

examples.english: The contents of the house were auctioned to pay off the family's debts.
examples.chinese: 家中物品被拍卖掉用来清偿家里的债务。

examples.english: The buyer did not attend the auction, but sent a representative to place the bids.
examples.chinese: 买主没有出席拍卖会，而是派了一名代理人来出价。

examples.english: Sotheby's decided to hold auctions in Japan twice a year.
examples.chinese: 苏富比决定在日本一年举行两次拍卖。

examples.english: Bikes that have not been claimed by the owners will be sold at a public auction January 11.
examples.chinese: 无人认领的自行车将于1月11日举行公开拍卖。

examples.english: How much do you think the painting would fetch at auction?
examples.chinese: 你觉得这幅画在拍卖会上可以卖多少钱？

examples.english: After months trying to sell the farm, they decided to put it up for auction.
examples.chinese: 这家农场几个月都没卖掉，他们决定将它拍卖。

examples.english: One of the world's finest collections of vintage cars is to be put up for auction.
examples.chinese: 一辆世界上最好的古典老爷汽车之一将要进行拍卖。

examples.english: A collection of prints and paintings by Picasso came under the hammer at Sotheby's yesterday.
examples.chinese: 毕加索的一批版画和油画昨天在苏富比进行拍卖。

centers.english: someone who sells things in a shop

examples.english: The shopkeeper chased the boys out of his shop, accusing them of stealing.
examples.chinese: 店老板追着把这几个男孩赶出店，说他们偷东西。

examples.english: Many immigrants have been highly successful as shopkeepers.
examples.chinese: 许多移民开店做生意都非常成功。

examples.english: Proprietor Ginny Gavin serves fresh croissants and fruit at breakfast to guests at her inn.
examples.chinese: 老板娘金妮·加文用新鲜的羊角面包和水果做早餐招待住在她客店里的客人。

examples.english: Dan Conrad, the proprietor of Conrad's Bookstore, says that small stores such as his offer service and convenience.
examples.chinese: 康拉德书店的老板丹·康拉德说，像他这样的小书店既提供服务，又提供便利。

examples.english: Local merchants have had trouble with vandals breaking windows.
examples.chinese: 当地店主苦恼的是，常有流氓砸打橱窗。

examples.english: Merchants say sales have not been affected by the road repairs.
examples.chinese: 店主说生意没有受到修路的影响。

examples.english: Rowan worked as a sales assistant in a Beverly Hills shopping mall.
examples.chinese: 罗恩在贝弗利山庄的一个购物中心里当营业员。

examples.english: She's a shop assistant in the shoe department.
examples.chinese: 她是皮鞋部的营业员。

examples.english: Get advice from a knowledgeable sales clerk.
examples.chinese: 问问营业员的意见，他们很在行。

examples.english: I worked for a while as a salesman for a big computer corporation.
examples.chinese: 我给一家大型电脑公司当过一会儿推销员。

examples.english: Gail has been the firm's top saleswoman for the last two years.
examples.chinese: 近两年盖尔一直是公司的最佳推销员。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: The company has a sales staff of 600.
examples.chinese: 这家公司有600名销售人员。

examples.english: Customers liked the personal service the sales staff provided.
examples.chinese: 顾客喜欢销售人员提供的贴心服务。

examples.english: Outside the theatre, there was a row of flower vendors.
examples.chinese: 剧院外面有一排卖花的小贩。

examples.english: Frank stopped to buy the evening paper from a news vendor.
examples.chinese: 弗兰克停下来向报贩买了一份晚报。

examples.english: Flags sold by street vendors fluttered in the crowd of about 5,000.
examples.chinese: 街头小贩卖出的旗子在五千人左右的人群中飘扬。

centers.english: a person or company that sells goods, shares etc
centers.chinese: 出售商品、股票等的人或公司

examples.english: Our products are sold through the Body Shop and other well-known retailers.
examples.chinese: 我们的产品通过美体小铺等一些著名的零售店出售。

examples.english: Retailers face their slowest business period in January and February.
examples.chinese: 1月份和2月份是零售商生意最不景气的时候。

examples.english: Talbot's is a women's retailer with 20 stores in California.
examples.chinese: 塔尔博特是一家女性用品零售公司，在加利福尼亚州有20家分店。

examples.english: One high-street retailer has gone out of business.
examples.chinese: 有一家大型零售公司倒闭了。

examples.english: The early morning market is for wholesalers only, the general public have to wait until later in the day.
examples.chinese: 早市只为批发商开放，普通民众必须等到晚些时候。

examples.english: Fisherman are involved in a price dispute with fish wholesalers.
examples.chinese: 渔民和鱼商之间出现了价格纠纷。

examples.english: She was born in 1432, the daughter of a wealthy London merchant.
examples.chinese: 她出生于1432年，是伦敦一名富商的女儿。

examples.english: an international arms merchant
examples.chinese: 跨国军火商

examples.english: Make sure you buy your used car from an authorised dealer.
examples.chinese: 旧车一定要到获授权的经销商那里去购买。

examples.english: ‘The market has been strong,’ said one dealer.
examples.chinese: “市场处于强势。”一名经销商说。

examples.english: She bought the painting from a Swiss art dealer.
examples.chinese: 她是从瑞士一位艺术品交易商那里买来这幅画的。

examples.english: According to a senior currency dealer, the pound is likely to continue to rise against the dollar.
examples.chinese: 根据一位资深货币交易商说，英镑兑美元的汇价有可能继续上升。

examples.english: Traders enter the amount of stock they want to buy or sell, and the computer calculates a price.
examples.chinese: 交易者输入他们想要买卖的股票数量，电脑便会计算出一个价格。

examples.english: Montreal was founded by French fur traders in the 17th century.
examples.chinese: 蒙特利尔是法国皮货商在17世纪建立的。

examples.english: Bond traders worried about inflation have driven up interest rates on long-term bonds.
examples.chinese: 债券交易员担心通货膨胀抬升了长期债券的利率。

examples.english: Both buyer and seller should agree on the terms before the contract is signed.
examples.chinese: 买卖双方应该在合同签署以前商定条款。

centers.english: someone who sells things illegally

examples.english: Dealers were selling heroin outside the stadium.
examples.chinese: 毒贩在体育馆外面兜售海洛因。

examples.english: Drug pushers have been warned to stay away from the club.
examples.chinese: 卖毒品的人已经被警告远离这个俱乐部。

examples.english: Residents complain of cocaine and heroin dealers selling on the streets outside their homes.
examples.chinese: 居民投诉说，有毒贩在他们家门外的街头上卖可卡因和海洛因。

examples.english: Touts were selling tickets to the match for £50 or more.
examples.chinese: 票贩子们把比赛的票卖到50英镑或者更高。

examples.english: Organisers of the concert were worried there would be trouble from ticket touts.
examples.chinese: 音乐会的举办方担心会有票贩子的问题。

examples.english: Scalpers wanted $150 for seats that normally sell for $40.
examples.chinese: 一般卖40美元的票，票贩子要卖到150美元。

centers.english: something that is sold

examples.english: The store sells a wide range of goods.
examples.chinese: 这家商店出售各式各样的商品。

examples.english: The cost of almost all goods and services soared when price controls were removed.
examples.chinese: 取消价格限制之后，几乎所有的商品和服务费用都在飞涨。

examples.english: We import a lot of electrical goods from Japan.
examples.chinese: 我们从日本进口许多电器商品。

examples.english: There is less demand now for products like coal and steel.
examples.chinese: 现在煤和钢等产品的需求没有以前大了。

examples.english: The new product took more than three years to develop before being put on the market.
examples.chinese: 这种新产品投入市场之前开发了三年多时间。

examples.english: The company manufactures and delivers paper and paper products.
examples.chinese: 这家公司生产并运送纸张和纸制品。

examples.english: The merchandise is attractively displayed and the assistants are friendly and helpful.
examples.chinese: 商品陈列得很漂亮，店员态度友好，服务周到。

examples.english: The fire at the warehouse destroyed merchandise valued at over $2 million.
examples.chinese: 仓库的那场火灾烧毁了价值二百多万美元的商品。

examples.english: The value of China's exports to the US rose by over 50% last year.
examples.chinese: 去年中国对美国的出口额增长了50%以上。

examples.english: Britain's total exports to the other EU member states now exceed imports.
examples.chinese: 现在英国对其他欧盟成员国的出口总量超过了进口量。

examples.english: The country's main export is coal.
examples.chinese: 该国的主要出口商品是煤。

centers.english: to make something available for people to buy

expressions.text: put something on the market/come/go on the market

examples.english: We put our house on the market last September and we still haven't sold it.
examples.chinese: 我们去年9月把房子投放市场出售，到现在还没卖掉。

examples.english: The drug came on the market in the late 1990s and has been widely prescribed.
examples.chinese: 这种药于20世纪90年代末投放市场，并广泛使用。

examples.english: The car will probably sell for around $50,000 when it goes on the market.
examples.chinese: 这款汽车上市将能卖到50,000美元左右。

examples.english: Most wines are left to mature before they are put on the market.
examples.chinese: 多数葡萄酒都留待酿熟后才上市出售。

examples.english: The historic property has been put up for sale.
examples.chinese: 这个具历史意义的建筑在出售。

examples.english: After the merger, parts of the business are likely to be put up for sale.
examples.chinese: 合并之后，公司的有些部分可能要出售。

examples.english: Kodak brought out a camera which stores up to 100 images on a computer disc.
examples.chinese: 柯达公司推出一款相机，在一张电脑磁盘上能储存多达100张图片。

examples.english: The two cookbooks have sold well, and Doubleday is bringing them out in new paperback editions.
examples.chinese: 这两本烹饪书销量很好，道布尔迪公司正要推出新的平装本。

examples.english: Fiat launched a tiny ‘city car’ especially for Europe's narrow, crowded streets.
examples.chinese: 菲亚特公司推出了一款迷你型的“城市汽车”，是专为欧洲狭小而拥挤的街道设计的。

examples.english: Fezza, the clothes designer, launched his first collection in 1980.
examples.chinese: 1980年，服装设计师费扎推出了他的第一个系列。

examples.english: Ladybird publish books for young children.
examples.chinese: 瓢虫公司出版幼儿图书。

examples.english: Amateur Photographer is published every Tuesday.
examples.chinese: 《业余摄影师》每周二出版。

examples.english: Rowling's latest Harry Potter novel sold millions of copies as soon as it was published.
examples.chinese: 罗琳最新的《哈利·波特》小说一出版就卖了几百万册。

examples.english: Since the publication of her book, she's received thousands of letters from women who have had similar experiences.
examples.chinese: 自她的书出版以来，她收到了数千封来信，都是有相似经历的妇女写来的。

examples.english: None of the big UK publishers wanted to do a paperback version of the book.
examples.chinese: 英国没有一家大的出版公司愿意出版这本书的平装版。

examples.english: Her new album will be released at the end of the month.
examples.chinese: 她的新专辑将于本月底发行。

examples.english: Carrey's new comedy is due to be released in the US very soon.
examples.chinese: 凯瑞的新喜剧片很快就要在美国上映。

examples.english: The band's latest release record that has been released
examples.chinese: 该乐队的最新唱片应该于周五有售。

centers.english: available for people to buy

examples.english: There are several houses for sale in our street.
examples.chinese: 我们这街上有好几幢房子出售。

examples.english: There was ‘for sale’ notice in the car's window.
examples.chinese: 车窗里有“待售”的告示。

examples.english: The festival will have food and crafts for sale, games for children, and music.
examples.chinese: 这次节庆活动有食品和手工艺品出售，还有儿童玩的游戏、音乐等。

examples.english: The house has been up for sale for months.
examples.chinese: 房子已上市数月了。

examples.english: Several subsidiary businesses are up for sale.
examples.chinese: 有几家子公司在出售。

examples.english: These cameras are on sale in most electrical stores.
examples.chinese: 这些相机在大多数电器商店里都有售。

examples.english: The new model Toyota goes on sale next month.
examples.chinese: 新款的丰田汽车将于下月上市。

examples.english: It's one of the cheapest computers on the market.
examples.chinese: 这是市场上最便宜的电脑之一。

examples.english: There may be better shoes on the market, but this is the one the kids want.
examples.chinese: 市场上也许是有更好的鞋子，但小孩子要的就是这种。

examples.english: The house was on the market for $475,000.
examples.chinese: 这房子在市场上卖475,000美元。

centers.english: to stop selling something

examples.english: The mineral water was taken off the market while tests were being made.
examples.chinese: 这种矿泉水在做检测的时候被撤出了市场。

examples.english: Sales of the newspaper were so poor that it was taken off the market within a couple of months.
examples.chinese: 这份报纸的销量很差，不到几个月就撤出市场了。

examples.english: The Federal Drug Administration has said that all products containing the additive must be taken off the market immediately.
examples.chinese: 美国食品和药物管理局说，凡是含这种添加剂的产品都必须立即撤出市场。

examples.english: Newsagents across the country have withdrawn the magazine after numerous complaints from women's groups.
examples.chinese: 接到妇女团体的诸多投诉之后，全国各地的报刊经销店都收回了这份杂志。

examples.english: After two children had been hurt, the company was forced to withdraw the toy from store shelves.
examples.chinese: 两名儿童受伤以后，这家公司被迫把这种玩具从货架上撤了下来。

examples.english: Christmas decorations were withdrawn from sale yesterday following a fire-risk warning.
examples.chinese: 昨天发出了火灾隐患警告之后，圣诞节装饰品都停止销售了。

examples.english: Stores have agreed to the withdrawal of the offending videos.
examples.chinese: 各家商店都已同意收回这些带有冒犯性的录像带。

examples.english: Thousands of car baby-seats have had to be recalled after a fault was discovered in the safety harness.
examples.chinese: 安全带发现有问题之后，有数千个汽车婴儿座须要收回。

examples.english: The company voluntarily recalled about 11,000 of the devices to check them for defects.
examples.chinese: 该公司主动收回约11,000个这种装置，以检查是否有缺陷。

examples.english: They put a notice in the press ordering the recall of all the baby food that might have been contaminated.
examples.chinese: 他们在报上登了一则通知，下令收回所有可能受到污染的婴儿食物。

centers.english: the amount of something that is sold

examples.english: A big price increase led to a fall in sales.
examples.chinese: 大幅涨价使销量下滑。

examples.english: Sales of the book have been astonishing.
examples.chinese: 这本书的销量令人称奇。

examples.english: Coupons for discounts on certain products have increased sales of those products.
examples.chinese: 某些产品的折扣券使该等产品的销量增加了。

examples.english: Turnover at the two restaurants was about $7.4 million this year.
examples.chinese: 今年这两家餐厅的营业额约为740万美元。

examples.english: Recently the company has been trying to increase its turnover by diversifying into other fields.
examples.chinese: 近来这家公司试图扩大经营范围至其他领域，以此来增加营业额。

centers.english: when a lot of something is sold

examples.english: Books that don't sell are sent back to the publishers.
examples.chinese: 没有销路的图书就退还给出版商。

examples.english: The handcrafted rocking horses have sold well across the United States.
examples.chinese: 这种手工制的木马畅销全美。

examples.english: The last model didn't sell as well as they'd expected.
examples.chinese: 上一个型号卖得不如他们预期的好。

examples.english: Agatha Christie is said to be the world's best-selling author.
examples.chinese: 阿加莎·克里斯蒂据说是世界上最畅销的作家。

examples.english: a list of the week's best-selling music and children's videos
examples.chinese: 本周最畅销的音乐和儿童录像清单

examples.english: Chanel Number 5 is the best-selling scent of all time.
examples.chinese: 香奈儿5号一直是最畅销的香水。

examples.english: Several TV movies have been based on best sellers by Danielle Steel.
examples.chinese: 已有好几部电视电影是根据丹尼尔·斯蒂尔的畅销小说拍成的。

examples.english: Every year there's a toy that becomes a hard-to-find best-seller.
examples.chinese: 每年都有一种玩具成为市面难求的畅销品。

examples.english: All four Harry Potter books are currently on the bestseller list.
examples.chinese: 目前四本《哈利·波特》都在畅销书榜上。

examples.english: His latest album has outsold all his other records put together.
examples.chinese: 他的最新专辑比他其余所有唱片加起来都卖得好。

examples.english: Chardonnay continues to outsell other wines.
examples.chinese: 霞多丽白葡萄酒的销量继续超越其他葡萄酒。

examples.english: Grisham's new book is selling like hot cakes.
examples.chinese: 格里沙姆的新书很畅销。

examples.english: Since the heatwave started, ice cream vendors have been doing a roaring trade.
examples.chinese: 进入酷暑以后，卖冰淇淋的生意很兴隆。

examples.english: The shops outside the station were doing a roaring trade in umbrellas.
examples.chinese: 车站外面的店铺雨伞生意很兴隆。

centers.english: when all of something has been sold

examples.english: I went to the store to get some bread but they had sold out.
examples.chinese: 我到商店里去买面包，但是已经卖完了。

examples.english: Sunday newspapers often sell out by 10 o'clock.
examples.chinese: 星期日报经常到10点钟就卖完了。

examples.english: They opened at 8 o'clock, and by 8.30 they had sold out of tickets for the big game.
examples.chinese: 他们8点钟开门，到8点半就卖完了这场大赛的门票。

examples.english: We couldn't get tickets anywhere - the show was completely sold out.
examples.chinese: 我们哪里也买不到票，演出门票全部售完了。

examples.english: The band's European tour was a sell-out.
examples.chinese: 该乐队的欧洲巡回演出场场座满。

examples.english: The Mariners beat the Angels in front of a sell-out crowd.
examples.chinese: 水手队在满场观众面前打败了天使队。

examples.english: The scooters are popular and are often out of stock.
examples.chinese: 这种踏板车很畅销，经常卖光。

examples.english: We're out of stock, but we can put one on order for you.
examples.chinese: 我们没有现货了，但是可以为你订购。

examples.english: Flights to the US are all booked up this time of year.
examples.chinese: 每年这个时候去美国的航班都订满。

examples.english: Popular campsites are fully booked on weekends for most of the summer.
examples.chinese: 夏天大部分时间的周末，一些热门营地都全部订满。

examples.english: All the hotels in the area are booked up months in advance.
examples.chinese: 这地区所有旅馆都是提前几个月就订满了。

centers.english: to buy something

examples.english: I bought a new dress today at Macy's.
examples.chinese: 今天我在梅西百货商店买了件新连衣裙。

examples.english: John makes his living buying and selling used cars.
examples.chinese: 约翰靠买卖旧车谋生。

examples.english: The painting was bought by a museum in New York.
examples.chinese: 这幅画被纽约一家博物馆买了。

examples.english: Keith was going to buy me a ring, but now he says he wants to buy me a watch instead.
examples.chinese: 基思本来要给我买只戒指，但现在他说要给我买个手表。

examples.english: If you don't have enough money for the pen, I'll buy it for you.
examples.chinese: 如果你不够钱买钢笔的话，我买给你吧。

examples.english: I wouldn't buy anything from him - I don't trust him.
examples.chinese: 我不会从他那里买任何东西—我不信任他。

examples.english: The ranch, which was originally bought for $20,000, is now valued at over $2 million.
examples.chinese: 这个原来以两万美元买下的农场，现在的价值估计超过两百万元。

examples.english: Let me get the drinks. It's my turn to pay.
examples.chinese: 我来买饮料吧。这次轮到我付钱。

examples.english: Did you remember to get the bread?
examples.chinese: 你记得买面包吗？

examples.english: Guess what he got her for her birthday - an iron!
examples.chinese: 猜猜他给她买了什么生日礼物—一只熨斗！

examples.english: I'm going to get one of those video games for Hillary.
examples.chinese: 我打算买一套这种电子游戏送给希拉里。

examples.english: What do you think of this leather jacket? I got it for $40 on sale.
examples.chinese: 这件皮夹克你觉得怎么样？我在大减价时花了40美元买的。

examples.english: Foreign investors are not permitted to purchase land.
examples.chinese: 外国投资者不得购买土地。

examples.english: If this product does not give complete satisfaction, please return it to the manufacturer stating when and where it was purchased.
examples.chinese: 如果这件产品不能令您完全满意，请将其退还给制造商，并说明产品的购买日期和地点。

examples.english: The loan was supposed to be used for the purchase of a house.
examples.chinese: 这笔贷款本应用来购买房屋的。

examples.english: Readers have snapped up nearly 200,000 copies of the book.
examples.chinese: 读者踊跃购买，此书售出了差不多有20万册。

examples.english: The best bargains tend to be snapped up immediately.
examples.chinese: 最划算的便宜货往往会很快被人抢购一空。

examples.english: If you see one for under $100, snap it up!
examples.chinese: 如果你看到定价不到100美元的，就马上把它买下来！

examples.english: He's hoping to pick up a few bargains at the sales.
examples.chinese: 他希望在大减价中买到几件便宜货。

examples.english: It's just a little thing I picked up when I was in Kathmandu.
examples.chinese: 那不过是我在加德满都时碰巧买到的小玩意儿。

examples.english: That picture? Oh, I picked it up last week at a little shop downtown.
examples.chinese: 那幅画吗？哦，那是我上周在市区一家小商店里碰巧买到的。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive / intransitive verb

examples.english: We splashed out on a bottle of champagne to celebrate her promotion.
examples.chinese: 我们花了很多钱买了瓶香槟以庆祝她得到晋升。

examples.english: Let's splurge and have the steak.
examples.chinese: 我们就奢侈一回，吃牛排吧。

examples.english: In 1998 the business was acquired by a Dutch company.
examples.chinese: 该企业于1998年被一家荷兰公司收购。

examples.english: The statue was acquired at great expense by the City Corporation.
examples.chinese: 该塑像被城市公司以高价收购。

examples.english: Robinson spent $20 million to acquire the symphony hall.
examples.chinese: 鲁宾逊花了2,000万美金购得这座交响乐厅。

examples.english: The National Gallery has set aside £10 million for the acquisition of the painting.
examples.chinese: 国立艺术馆拨出了1,000万英镑收购这幅画作。

centers.english: to buy a lot of something
