examples.english: You've been buying a lot of clothes recently. Have you decided to change your image?
examples.chinese: 你最近买了许多衣服。是不是决定了要改变形象？

examples.english: We've bought lots of food and drink, so it should be a really good party.
examples.chinese: 我们已经买了许多食品和饮料，所以这个晚会大家应该可以玩得十分开心。

examples.english: The supermarkets are full of people stocking up for the New Year's holiday.
examples.chinese: 超级市场里挤满了为过新年备年货的人。

examples.english: We might as well stock up while we're here - it'll save us having to come back.
examples.chinese: 我们既然到了这里就不妨多买点东西—这样我们可以省得再来一次。

examples.english: We always stock up on cheap cigarettes when we go to Holland.
examples.chinese: 我们每次到荷兰去都大量购买廉价香烟。

examples.english: We always buy in bulk. It is so much more economical.
examples.chinese: 我们总是大批购买。这样经济得多。

examples.english: Today more shoppers are using coupons and buying items in bulk.
examples.chinese: 如今更多顾客都用优惠券，买起东西来都是大批量的。

examples.english: Bulk buying has enabled the company to cut costs.
examples.chinese: 大批量采购使公司降低了成本。

examples.english: Mark buys the earrings wholesale and then sells them for a profit.
examples.chinese: 马克批量购买耳环，再把它们转售图利。

examples.english: Let's see if we can get them wholesale and save ourselves a few dollars.
examples.chinese: 我们看看能不能批量购买，这样可以省下几块钱。

examples.english: The shopkeeper buys his fruit and vegetables at wholesale prices.
examples.chinese: 店主以批发价购买水果和蔬菜。

examples.english: In the last five years development agencies have bought up almost all the land in the area.
examples.chinese: 过去的五年内，开发商几乎买下了这地区的所有土地。

examples.english: There weren't any good seats left for the game - some big company had bought them all up.
examples.chinese: 这场比赛的好座位已一个不剩，有家大公司把好座位都买下了。

examples.english: The United States has to import some of its oil.
examples.chinese: 美国所用的原油有部分必须依靠进口。

examples.english: Most of the wines served in this restaurant are imported from France.
examples.chinese: 这家餐馆供应的酒大部分是从法国进口的。

examples.english: In 1999 our imports goods that were imported greatly exceeded exports.
examples.chinese: 1999年，我国的进口商品大大超过出口。

examples.english: People had to buy in candles during the electricity strike.
examples.chinese: 电厂工人罢工期间，人们不得不大量购买蜡烛。

examples.english: Villagers join together to buy enough food in to last throughout the winter.
examples.chinese: 村民们一起购买足够的食品过冬。

centers.english: to buy something for someone else

examples.english: As it's your birthday, I thought I'd treat you.
examples.chinese: 你的生日到了，我想请你吃饭。

examples.english: Glen treated Cathy to dinner at one of the best restaurants in town.
examples.chinese: 格伦请凯茜到镇上最好的一家饭店吃晚饭。

examples.english: Let's all go to a movie - my treat.
examples.chinese: 我们大家看电影去吧—我请客。

examples.english: Don't worry about the price - this meal's on me.
examples.chinese: 别管价钱—这顿饭我请客。

examples.english: The manager apologized and told us our drinks would be on the house for the rest of the evening.
examples.chinese: 经理道歉之后告诉我们说，当晚余下时间我们喝的酒由他们请客。

examples.english: I'll get this round. What would you like?
examples.chinese: 这一轮我来请。你们想喝点什么？

examples.english: Jack always leaves when it's his turn to buy a round.
examples.chinese: 每次轮到杰克请大家喝一杯的时候，他就会走开。

centers.english: to go to shops in order to buy things

examples.english: Let's meet in town. We can have lunch and go shopping.
examples.chinese: 我们在城里见面吧。我们用了午餐去逛商店。

examples.english: I'm going shopping now. Do you want anything?
examples.chinese: 我正要去购物。你要买什么东西吗？

examples.english: On Saturdays we usually do the shopping and clean the house.
examples.chinese: 周六我们通常会去购物和打扫屋子。

examples.english: She sent her husband out to do the week's shopping.
examples.chinese: 她让丈夫去买了一周需要的东西。

examples.english: I did all my shopping yesterday.
examples.chinese: 我昨天把日常需要的物品都买齐了。

examples.english: We need to go grocery shopping - do you have the check book?
examples.chinese: 我们要去买些食品杂货，你有支票簿吗？

expressions.text: go to the shops go to the stor

examples.english: ‘Where's Julie?’ ‘She's gone to the shops.’
examples.chinese: “朱莉在哪里？”“她买东西去了。”

examples.english: If you go to the store, could you get some milk?
examples.chinese: 你去买东西的话，就买些牛奶好吗?

examples.english: Mr Parker, my next-door neighbour, is getting old and I sometimes go to the shops for him.
examples.chinese: 住在我隔壁的帕克先生已上了年纪，所以我有时替他去商店买东西。

examples.english: I usually shop at Safeway. It's just around the corner from my house.
examples.chinese: 我通常在西夫韦购物。该店就在我家不远的拐角处。

examples.english: When she moved here, she had never shopped in a supermarket before.
examples.chinese: 她搬到这儿住之前从未在超级市场买过东西。

examples.english: If you shop around you could probably get the camera a lot cheaper.
examples.chinese: 你要是货比三家，就能以便宜得多的价钱买到这台相机。

examples.english: I spent a couple of weeks shopping around for the lowest insurance rates.
examples.chinese: 我花了几周时间逐家公司比较，希望买到最便宜的保险。

examples.english: We spent the morning window shopping at all the antique stores.
examples.chinese: 我们一上午都在逛街浏览所有古玩店的橱窗。

centers.english: someone who buys goods or services

examples.english: We don't get many customers on Mondays - Saturday is our busiest day.
examples.chinese: 周一我们的顾客不多——周六是最忙的一天。

examples.english: The barman was serving the last customer of the evening.
examples.chinese: 酒吧侍应在接待当晚最后一位顾客。

examples.english: Ford has launched a big sales campaign in an effort to bring in new customers.
examples.chinese: 福特公司展开了一项大型的促销活动，旨在发掘新顾客。

examples.english: The Defense Department is one of Lockheed's biggest regular customers.
examples.chinese: 国防部是洛克希德公司最大的固定客户之一。

examples.english: The streets were crowded with Christmas shoppers.
examples.chinese: 街上挤满了购买圣诞物品的人。

examples.english: Mr Langston normally meets with clients in the afternoon.
examples.chinese: 兰斯顿先生一般都在下午与客户见面。

examples.english: Elkins assured the judge that neither of his clients had a criminal record.
examples.chinese: 埃尔金斯向法官保证说他的委托人都没有犯罪记录。

examples.english: The firm is one of our oldest clients - we don't want to lose them.
examples.chinese: 这家公司是我们最老的主顾之一——我们可不想失去他们。

examples.english: We couldn't find a buyer for our house, so we weren't able to move after all.
examples.chinese: 我们的房子找不到买主，所以最终没法搬家。

examples.english: They've had a lot of enquiries about the company - there's no shortage of potential buyers.
examples.chinese: 他们接到了大量对那家公司的询问，看来不愁找不到潜在的买主。

examples.english: Consumers are demanding more environmentally friendly products.
examples.chinese: 消费者要求更多的环保产品。

examples.english: The consumer is interested in high quality goods, not just low prices.
examples.chinese: 消费者感兴趣的是高质量的产品，而非仅仅是低廉的价格。

examples.english: The hotel's clientele includes diplomats and Hollywood celebrities.
examples.chinese: 这家酒店的顾客有外交官和好莱坞名人。

examples.english: Madame Zara caters for a very select clientele.
examples.chinese: “扎拉夫人”商店为一批高级的顾客提供服务。

examples.english: The magazine is aimed at the youth market.
examples.chinese: 这份杂志面向年轻人的市场。

examples.english: Without research we can't be sure of the size of our market or even who our market is.
examples.chinese: 没有市场调查我们就无法确定我们的市场大小，甚至连哪些人是我们的顾客也不知道。

examples.english: The market for Internet-based products has grown dramatically in recent years.
examples.chinese: 近年来互联网相关产品的市场增长迅速。

centers.english: what you have to pay for something
centers.chinese: 为购买某物所支付的金额

examples.english: We'll make sure you have the operation, whatever the cost.
examples.chinese: 不管费用多少，要确保你做手术。

examples.english: The cost of electricity has fallen in the last twelve months.
examples.chinese: 电费在过去的12个月里下跌了。

examples.english: Internet banking will considerably reduce the cost of doing business.
examples.chinese: 网络银行将会大大降低做生意的成本。

examples.english: The high cost of health care in the US is causing a great deal of concern.
examples.chinese: 美国高昂的医疗费用正在引起极大关注。

examples.english: If you lose the case, you will face substantial legal costs.
examples.chinese: 如果输了官司，你将面对大笔的诉讼费用。

examples.english: Many old people have to live in poverty because of the steady rise in the cost of living.
examples.chinese: 由于生活费用持续上涨，许多老年人不得不过着贫困的生活。

examples.english: IBM is continuing to cut costs in an effort to be more competitive.
examples.chinese: 为了提高竞争力，国际商业机器公司不断在削减成本。

examples.english: We will deliver and install your computer at no extra cost.
examples.chinese: 你的电脑由我们送货安装，不收取额外费用。

examples.english: £650,000 will be needed to cover the hospital's running costs during its first year.
examples.chinese: 第一年需要65万英镑来支付医院的经营费用。

examples.english: There's a great new clothes store on Main Street, and its prices seem very reasonable.
examples.chinese: 大街上新开了一家很棒的服装店，价格似乎十分合理。

examples.english: What's the price of a pack of cigarettes nowadays?
examples.chinese: 如今一包烟多少钱?

examples.english: Microsoft chairman Bill Gates said it was impossible to build a good computer for such a low price.
examples.chinese: 微软主席比尔·盖茨说，以这么低的价格制造优质电脑是不可能的。

examples.english: House prices rose by around 12% in the south-east last year.
examples.chinese: 去年，东南部的房价上涨了12%左右。

examples.english: They charge the same price for a takeaway as they do for eating in the restaurant.
examples.chinese: 他们那家餐馆，外卖和堂吃的价钱是一样的。

examples.english: I bought these jeans half price in a sale.
examples.chinese: 大减价时，我用半价买了这条牛仔裤。

examples.english: Apple was forced to cut prices sharply, reducing its profit margin.
examples.chinese: 苹果公司被迫大幅减价，降低了毛利率。

examples.english: The Japanese have raised prices just $8 a vehicle on average.
examples.chinese: 日本人平均每辆车只涨了八美元。

examples.english: Experts say they expect price rises to be gradual but persistent.
examples.chinese: 专家称，他们预计价格会持续缓慢上涨。

examples.english: The asking price for the 60-acre estate in Atlanta is $27 million.
examples.chinese: 亚特兰大那处60英亩地产的要价为2，700万美元。

examples.english: There's no charge for telephoning the operator.
examples.chinese: 打电话给接线员不收费。

examples.english: If your order comes to over $30, we will not make a delivery charge.
examples.chinese: 如果订单超过30美元，我们不收取送货费。

examples.english: There's an admission charge for adults, but children get into the museum free.
examples.chinese: 成年人要买门票，但小孩子进博物馆免费。

examples.english: Members and their guests are welcome to use the club's facilities at no extra charge.
examples.chinese: 请会员及他们的客人随意使用俱乐部的设施，不需另外付费。

examples.english: An additional charge of 15% will be added to your bill for service.
examples.chinese: 账单上要另加15%服务费。

examples.english: Some actors can ask a fee of around $1,000,000 a movie.
examples.chinese: 有些演员拍一部电影要价一百万美元左右。

examples.english: The doctor I saw charged a £100 fee for an initial consultation.
examples.chinese: 给我看病的医生收取100英镑初诊费。

examples.english: An accident on vacation can cost you a lot in medical fees.
examples.chinese: 度假时发生的意外会让你花掉大笔医疗费。

examples.english: The entrance fees to the park have gone up by 50%.
examples.chinese: 公园入场费涨了50%。

examples.english: We are able to offer a whole range of services at very reasonable rates.
examples.chinese: 我们可以提供全套服务，而且价格非常合理。

examples.english: Our shop assistants are paid an hourly rate of £5.50
examples.chinese: 我们店员的工资每小时5.50英镑。

examples.english: £150 is the going rate for tickets for the concert.
examples.chinese: 这场音乐会票价一般为150英镑。

examples.english: There is a fixed rate for the job, regardless of how long it takes.
examples.chinese: 干这工作报酬是固定的，不计时。

examples.english: I had to walk home because I didn't have enough money for the fare.
examples.chinese: 我只好走回家去，因为带的钱不够付车费。

examples.english: How much is the train fare from Toronto to Montreal?
examples.chinese: 多伦多到蒙特利尔的火车票多少钱？

examples.english: A one-week stay in Majorca costs $779 including air fare.
examples.chinese: 在马略卡岛住一周连机票要779美元。

examples.english: The biggest fare increases were on the Kansas City to Minneapolis line.
examples.chinese: 涨幅最大的是堪萨斯城至明尼阿波利斯航线。

examples.english: You have to pay tolls on many of the major roads in France.
examples.chinese: 在法国，许多主要公路都收取通行费。

examples.english: In 1871 they built a toll bridge from the mainland to the island.
examples.chinese: 1871年，他们建造了大陆至该岛的收费大桥。

examples.english: She pays £350 a month rent for a one-bedroomed apartment.
examples.chinese: 她每月支付350英镑租住一套一间卧室的公寓。

examples.english: Office rents are highest in the city centre.
examples.chinese: 市中心的办公室租金最高。

expressions.text: put up the rent/raise the rent

examples.english: If my landlord raises the rent again, I'll have to look for somewhere smaller.
examples.chinese: 要是房东再加房租的话，我得另找小一点的地方了。

examples.english: The rental on the TV includes maintenance and repairs.
examples.chinese: 电视机租金包括保养和修理费用。

examples.english: Car rental is $200 a week and you need a clean driving licence.
examples.chinese: 汽车租金每周200美元，你还需有一张无违章记录的驾驶执照。

centers.english: ways of saying or asking how much something costs
centers.chinese: 表示或询问某事物的价钱的说法

examples.english: How much does a house like that cost in America?
examples.chinese: 那样的房子在美国要多少钱？

examples.english: Tickets for the show cost £15 or £20.
examples.chinese: 演出票价为15英镑或20英镑。

examples.english: I stayed in a hotel in Paris which cost me $150 a night.
examples.chinese: 在巴黎我住在一家每晚150美元的酒店。

examples.english: Look at Frank's new Mercedes - it must have cost a fortune.
examples.chinese: 看看弗兰克的新梅赛德斯车—肯定花了很多钱。

expressions.text: it costs £10/$20/a lot etc to do something

examples.english: The Department of Education estimates that it will cost $17 billion to build the new schools.
examples.chinese: 教育部估算，建新校舍要耗费170亿美元。

examples.english: That's a beautiful rug -- how much did you pay for it?
examples.chinese: 那地毯真漂亮——你花了多少钱买的?

examples.english: By the way, how much does it cost to use the swimming pool?
examples.chinese: 顺便问一下，使用游泳池要多少钱？

examples.english: ‘I like your new shirt - how much was it?’ ‘It was only fifteen pounds.’
examples.chinese: “我很喜欢你的新衬衫—多少钱？”“只要15英镑。”

examples.english: I can't remember how much the flight cost. I think it was around $400.
examples.chinese: 我记不得机票多少钱了，我想是400美元左右。

examples.english: Surveys are being conducted in 10 European States at a cost of £50 million.
examples.chinese: 调查在十个欧洲国家开展，耗资5,000万英镑。

examples.english: A good quality saxophone will set you back at least £1000.
examples.chinese: 一支优质的萨克斯管会让你至少花1,000英镑。

examples.english: If she's hoping to buy a new sportscar, it's going to really set her back!
examples.chinese: 如果她想买辆新的跑车，那一定会让她破不少财的！

examples.english: The book, which is priced at £38, will be available in the shops from September.
examples.chinese: 该书定价38英镑，9月起就能在书店里买到。

examples.english: I watched a demonstration of their new cordless phone, priced at $350.
examples.chinese: 我观看了他们那台定价350美元的新款无绳电话的演示。

examples.english: Clothes with designer labels sell for ridiculous prices nowadays.
examples.chinese: 如今挂有设计师标签的服装都是天价。

examples.english: Houses in this area are selling for over $400,000.
examples.chinese: 这地区的房子售价在40万美元以上。

examples.english: Watches like that are going for about £15 in the market.
examples.chinese: 那样的手表在市场上卖15英镑左右。

examples.english: The painting finally went to a private collector for $60 million.
examples.chinese: 这幅画最后被一位私人收藏家以6,000万美元的价格买下。

examples.english: It's a very old car, but I'm still hoping it'll fetch around £200.
examples.chinese: 这辆车很旧了，但是我仍希望卖到200英镑左右。

examples.english: Van Gogh's "Sunflowers’ was expected to fetch more than $20 million.
examples.chinese: 梵高的《向日葵》大概能卖到两千多万美元。

examples.english: ‘The mechanic's just about finished working on your car, sir.’ ‘Thanks, and what's the damage?’
examples.chinese: “机械师就快把你的车修好了，先生。”“谢谢，一共多少钱？”

centers.english: to calculate how much something costs
centers.chinese: 计算某物的成本或给某物定价

examples.english: The project had been incorrectly costed and the money ran out before it could be completed.
examples.chinese: 工程成本计算有误，竣工前资金都已耗尽了。

examples.english: It would be a good idea to get the plan costed before presenting it to the board.
examples.chinese: 把计划提交给董事会之前先估算一下成本是明智的。

examples.english: Porsche said its new 911 Carrera 4s would be very competitively priced.
examples.chinese: 保时捷公司称其911卡雷拉4s型车定价将会极具竞争力。

examples.english: Please get your fruit and vegetables weighed and priced before you take them to the checkout.
examples.chinese: 去收银台前，请将水果和蔬菜称重标价。

examples.english: The final cost was £2000 higher than the original estimate.
examples.chinese: 最终的成本比原来估计高出2,000英镑。

examples.english: I've asked the builders to give us an estimate for fixing the roof.
examples.chinese: 我已要求建筑商估计给我们修屋顶的成本。

examples.english: Get a few quotations from different firms so that you can compare prices.
examples.chinese: 先向不同的公司寻求几个报价，这样你就能比较一下价格了。

centers.english: to take or ask for an amount of money in return for something you are selling

examples.english: Lawyers charge such high fees, but they never seem short of clients.
examples.chinese: 律师收费极高，但他们似乎从来不缺客户。

examples.english: My piano teacher charges £9 for a half hour class.
examples.chinese: 我的钢琴老师一堂半小时的课收费九英镑。

examples.english: Small shops charge much higher prices for the same products.
examples.chinese: 同样的商品，小店要价高得多。

examples.english: The cheapest doctor we could find charged us four hundred francs for a five minute examination.
examples.chinese: 我们能找到的收费最便宜的医生，检查五分钟，向我们收了400法郎。

examples.english: I might be interested in your TV. How much do you want for it?
examples.chinese: 我也许对你的电视机有兴趣。你要多少钱?

examples.english: Bob said he'd give Frank private guitar lessons, but he wanted $60 an hour.
examples.chinese: 鲍勃说他愿意当弗兰克的私人吉他教师—但他每小时要收60美元。

centers.english: to use money to buy things

examples.english: Everyone spends more at Christmas - it's an important time for business.
examples.chinese: 圣诞节的时候大家都会花更多的钱—这是一个重要的商机。

examples.english: During the recession, even the tourists weren't spending.
examples.chinese: 经济不景气的时候，连游客都不花钱了。

examples.english: I bought two skirts and a T-shirt and I only spent $50.
examples.chinese: 我买了两条裙子，一件T恤衫，只花了50美元。

examples.english: We spend about £85 a week on food.
examples.chinese: 我们一个星期大约花85英镑购买食物。

examples.english: The government has promised to spend more money on education.
examples.chinese: 政府承诺要花更多的钱在教育上。

examples.english: She spends most of her salary on clothes.
examples.chinese: 她的工资大部分都花在衣服上。

examples.english: They spend quite a lot of money each week on eating out.
examples.chinese: 他们每个星期花很多钱出去吃饭。

examples.english: Of course you have to pay more if you want to travel in the summer.
examples.chinese: 如果你想夏天出游当然得付更多的钱。

examples.english: What quality accommodation you get depends on how much you're prepared to pay.
examples.chinese: 住宿条件质量如何要看你愿意出多少钱。

examples.english: I like your new car - how much did you pay for it?
examples.chinese: 我喜欢你的新车—你花多少钱买的？

examples.english: They paid over $100 each for tickets.
examples.chinese: 他们每张票付了一百多美元。

examples.english: The set meal costs £15 but you have to pay extra for wine.
examples.chinese: 套餐是15英镑，但是葡萄酒的钱得另外付。

examples.english: £65! I don't want to pay out that much!
examples.chinese: 65英镑！我可不想出那么多钱!

examples.english: You have to pay out so much money for car repairs these days.
examples.chinese: 现在修车得花那么多钱。

examples.english: The idea of paying out half my salary on rent didn't sound too good.
examples.chinese: 要我花一半的工资支付房租,这好像不太好。

examples.english: Did you know that Eddy paid out nearly £2000 for his new computer?
examples.chinese: 你知道吗，埃迪买新电脑花了将近2,000英镑。

examples.english: ‘I don't really want to spend that much.’ ‘OK, how much are you prepared to give?’
examples.chinese: “我真的不想花这么多钱。”“好吧，那你愿意出多少？”

examples.english: Did they give you the asking price for the house?
examples.chinese: 他们出的是房子的要价吗?

examples.english: He said he'd give £40 for the painting, so I said yes.
examples.chinese: 他说他愿意出40英镑买这幅画，我就答应了。

examples.english: We had to break into our savings to pay the hospital fees.
examples.chinese: 我们只得动用存款支付医疗费。

examples.english: I really want to avoid having to break into the money I was saving for college.
examples.chinese: 我实在不想动用我为上大学而存的钱。

examples.english: I paid good money for that tennis racquet and it broke the first time I used it.
examples.chinese: 我花了不少钱买那网球拍，可第一次用它就坏了。

examples.english: What's the point of paying good money for a wedding dress when I'm only going to wear it once?
examples.chinese: 婚纱我只穿一次，花很多钱去买有什么意思呢？

centers.english: to spend a lot of money

examples.english: You don't have to spend a lot to be fashionable -- you just need a sense of style.
examples.chinese: 时髦不一定要花很多钱—你需要的是有时尚感。

examples.english: They must have spent a lot on their new kitchen. It's made of solid oak
examples.chinese: 他们的新厨房一定花了不少钱，都是用实心橡木做的。

examples.english: In recent years the company has spent a lot of money on new technology.
examples.chinese: 近年来公司在新技术上花了不少钱。

examples.english: The wedding was wonderful. Your parents obviously went to great expense.
examples.chinese: 婚礼非常隆重，你父母显然是花了大钱的。

expressions.text: go to great expense to do something

examples.english: Please let us have your comments on the plans for the new offices -- we're going to great expense to get everything just right.
examples.chinese: 请你给我们新办公室的布局提点意见—我们打算不惜花费全都要弄得很好。

examples.english: The organizers were told to spare no expense - this was going to be the biggest show on Earth.
examples.chinese: 筹办者被告知要不惜工本——这将是全球最大的一次展览会。

examples.english: ‘Go out and buy whatever you want,’ he said, ‘no expense spared!’
examples.chinese: “去，想买什么就买什么，”他说，“别舍不得花钱！”

examples.english: No, we can't afford to go to the bowling alley - I've forked out enough already today.
examples.chinese: 不行，我们没有钱去打保龄球了——今天我已经花了很多钱。

examples.english: Insurance companies are having to shell out millions of pounds to the victims of the floods.
examples.chinese: 保险公司将要向水灾的受灾者支付几百万英镑的赔款。

examples.english: I'm not shelling out any more money on this old car. It's not worth it!
examples.chinese: 我再也不想在这旧车上花钱了，不值得！

examples.english: I failed my driving test and Dad said that he wasn't forking out for any more lessons for me.
examples.chinese: 我的驾驶考试没有通过，爸爸说他不会再出钱让我学车了。

examples.english: Jilly and I decided to cheer ourselves up and go on a spending spree.
examples.chinese: 我和吉利决定去大买特买让自己开心开心。

examples.english: You haven't been on another spending spree, have you? What did you buy this time?
examples.chinese: 你又疯狂购物了？这一次又买了什么呢？

examples.english: Simon always ordered the best. It was obvious that money was no object.
examples.chinese: 西蒙总是要最好的，显然钱不是问题。

examples.english: Choose whatever outfit you want - money no object!
examples.chinese: 你想买什么衣服就买吧—别管多少钱！

examples.english: For several years they lived the high life with Hollywood stars and celebrities.
examples.chinese: 他们过了好几年优裕的生活，往来的都是好莱坞的明星和名流。

examples.english: You've been living the high life recently, haven't you! You're always going out to clubs and fancy restaurants.
examples.chinese: 你最近过得很豪华啊！出入的都是俱乐部和高级餐厅。

centers.english: to spend money quickly or carelessly

examples.english: In less than three years he had squandered the entire family fortune.
examples.chinese: 不到三年，他就把家里所有的财产都挥霍一空。

examples.english: There was no money to pay the rent. They'd already squandered the little that they had.
examples.chinese: 没有钱付房租了，仅有的一些钱他们也已经挥霍掉了。

examples.english: Here's £50 but don't just go and squander it on beer!
examples.chinese: 这里有50英镑，可是别拿去喝个啤酒就花光了！

examples.english: We blew $3000 on a trip to Barbados.
examples.chinese: 我们去一趟巴巴多斯花掉了3，000美元。

examples.english: He won £500,000 in the National Lottery, but he's already blown the lot.
examples.chinese: 他在全国彩票中了50万英镑，可是已经挥霍完了。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: I got through all of my money in less than a month and had to get my parents to send me more.
examples.chinese: 不到一个月的时间我就把所有的钱都花光了，不得不叫父母再寄一点给我。

examples.english: The hotel was really expensive. We went through $3000 in the first week.
examples.chinese: 这家酒店很昂贵，我们第一个星期就花掉了3,000美元。

expressions.text: spend money like water/like there's no tomorrow

examples.english: Richard spends money like there's no tomorrow! Where does he get it all from?
examples.chinese: 理查德花起钱来不考虑将来！他哪里来那么多钱?

examples.english: I don't trust myself with a credit card - I spend money like water as it is.
examples.chinese: 我身上不敢带信用卡—我花钱如流水。

centers.english: to spend less money

examples.english: We've had to cut down a lot since Craig lost his job - it's been very hard for us.
examples.chinese: 克雷格失业以后，我们不得不节俭许多—日子过得很艰难。

examples.english: She's already cut down on going out and buying clothes, but she doesn't have enough money to start paying off her debts.
examples.chinese: 她已经减少了出去约会和买衣服的花费，但还是不够钱还债。

examples.english: The department has overspent this year and we will have to cut down our expenses.
examples.chinese: 今年本部门已经超支，我们不得不削减费用。

examples.english: Sorry, I can't come out tonight - I'm trying to economize.
examples.chinese: 对不起，今天晚上我不能出来了—我在想办法省钱呢。

examples.english: We're economizing this year by having a cheaper vacation.
examples.chinese: 我们今年很节俭，去了一个比较便宜的地方度假。

examples.english: Families on low incomes are having to economize on food and heating costs.
examples.chinese: 低收入家庭只能在食物和暖气装置方面省钱。

examples.english: Chris's parents scrimped and saved so that he could go to college.
examples.chinese: 克里斯的父母节衣缩食供他上大学。

examples.english: I had hardly any money left and was scrimping and saving just to buy the bare necessities.
examples.chinese: 我的钱所剩无几，就省吃俭用，只买一些最必需的生活用品。

examples.english: Most people have to tighten their belts a little when they retire.
examples.chinese: 大多数人退休以后只得勒紧裤带过日子。

examples.english: Governments and companies are forced to tighten their belts during a recession.
examples.chinese: 经济衰退期间，政府和公司都不得不勒紧裤带过日子。

examples.english: We'll have to budget more carefully in the future. We've spent far more than we can afford.
examples.chinese: 我们今后得精打细算一点了，我们已经入不敷出了。

examples.english: By the time I had budgeted for food and rent I only had a few pounds left.
examples.chinese: 等到我计划好吃和住的费用时，口袋里已经所剩无几了。

centers.english: spending as little as possible

examples.english: Nearly all of our research had to be conducted on a shoestring.
examples.chinese: 我们几乎所有的研究都是靠极少的经费进行的。

examples.english: The paper started on a shoestring, but soon had a circulation of over 100,000 readers.
examples.chinese: 这家报社白手起家，但是发行量很快就超过了10万份。

examples.english: a shoestring budget
examples.chinese: 很拮据的预算

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: There's no point in skimping on essentials such as food and heating.
examples.chinese: 食物和暖气装置等基本的开销没有必要节省。

examples.english: A company will never get anywhere by skimping on training and technology.
examples.chinese: 一家公司如果在培训和开发技术方面舍不得花钱，就成不了大事。

examples.english: You only had to look at the houses to see that they'd been built on the cheap.
examples.chinese: 你看他们贪便宜造的是什么房子。

examples.english: It's impossible to provide good nursing care on the cheap.
examples.chinese: 需要提供良好的护理服务又少花钱是不可能的。

centers.english: someone who spends a lot of money carelessly

examples.english: $400 on a dress! That's a bit extravagant, isn't it?
examples.chinese: 一条连衣裙400美元！那太奢侈了吧。

examples.english: Rich and extravagant parents are spending more and more money on their children's parties.
examples.chinese: 有钱而又铺张的家长在孩子的聚会上花钱越来越多。

examples.english: She put the money in to a savings account so she wasn't tempted to spend it extravagantly.
examples.chinese: 她把钱存入一个储蓄账户，这样就不会动心乱花掉了。

examples.english: I remember him as a charming but irresponsible spendthrift.
examples.chinese: 他在我的印象中是个很有魅力的人，但是挥金如土。

examples.english: She was by no means a spendthrift, but somehow all the money disappeared anyway.
examples.chinese: 她绝不是个花钱没有节制的人，可是钱都不知道到哪里去了。

examples.english: I didn't trust him at all. He was a big spender, that's all.
examples.chinese: 我根本不相信他，他花钱太大手大脚，就是这个原因。

examples.english: This is the time of year when all the big spenders pour into Las Vegas hotels and casinos.
examples.chinese: 每年这个时候，所有的挥金如土的人都涌到拉斯韦加斯的酒店和赌场去了。

centers.english: someone who spends money carefully

examples.english: Mrs Jones was a very thrifty woman who never wasted anything.
examples.chinese: 琼斯夫人非常节俭，从来不浪费。

examples.english: By being thrifty and shopping wisely you can feed an entire family on as little as $100 a week.
examples.chinese: 节俭一点，买东西算精明一点，这样每星期用少至100美元就可以养活一家人了。

examples.english: The old values of thrift being thrifty and hard work seem to be things of the past.
examples.chinese: 节俭和勤劳这些旧价值观似乎已经成了往事。

examples.english: I wouldn't say he was mean -- he's just careful.
examples.chinese: 我不会说他小气—他只是用钱谨慎而已。

examples.english: Once you've spent your allowance there won't be any more. You must learn to be more careful with money.
examples.chinese: 津贴一旦用完了你就不会再有了一你得学会精打细算。

examples.english: I'm trying to be more economical when I go shopping, and only buying what I really need.
examples.chinese: 我购物时尽量节约，只有真正需要时才买。

examples.english: Tim's a very economical person,. He always looks around for the best buys.
examples.chinese: 蒂姆是个很节约的人，他总是货比三家，买最合算的东西。

examples.english: By shopping economically, you can save a lot of money on your weekly bill.
examples.chinese: 买东西节约点，每个星期可以省不少钱。

examples.english: He was very frugal, and would often use a tea bag three or four times over.
examples.chinese: 他非常节约，一个茶包可以用上三四次。

examples.english: The monks lead a frugal life, allowing themselves only the bare essentials.
examples.chinese: 僧侣们过着节俭的生活，只允许自己使用最基本的生活必需品。

examples.english: We moved to a little house in the country and tried to live as frugally as possible.
examples.chinese: 我们搬到乡下的一所小房子去，过着尽可能节俭的生活。

centers.english: someone who hates spending money

examples.english: Don't be so stingy! It's your turn to buy me a drink.
examples.chinese: 别这么小气！该轮到你来请我喝酒了。

examples.english: It's no use asking him - he's too stingy to give money to charity.
examples.chinese: 问他要是没有用的—他吝啬得很，不会捐钱做慈善的。

examples.english: Rick's so mean he never even buys his wife a birthday present.
examples.chinese: 里克很小气，甚至从来不给妻子买生日礼物。

examples.english: My father was a mean old man who resented every penny he spent on us.
examples.chinese: 我父亲是个吝啬的老头，花在我们身上的每一分钱他都很不情愿。

examples.english: Uncle Matt was really cheap - he used to stay with us for weeks, and he never paid for anything.
examples.chinese: 马特叔叔真吝啬，以前他在我们家一待就是几个星期，却从不肯出一分钱。

examples.english: He was known to have made a fortune on the stock market, but was nonetheless notoriously tight-fisted.
examples.chinese: 都说他在股市上发了财，然而却是尽人皆知一毛不拔的人。

examples.english: Mr Henny was a miser who had thousands of pounds hidden away under his bed.
examples.chinese: 亨尼先生是个守财奴，他有好几千英镑的现金藏在床底下。

centers.english: the amount that you spend

examples.english: The government intends to cut its expenditure by 10% next year.
examples.chinese: 政府打算明年削减开支10%。

examples.english: The answer to inadequate health care is to increase public spending, not reduce it.
examples.chinese: 医疗保健资源不足，其解决办法是要增加公共支出，而不是减少公共支出。

examples.english: Company spending on staff benefits has been cut dramatically in recent years.
examples.chinese: 近年来，公司在员工福利方面的支出大大减少了。

examples.english: What are your annual fuel costs?
examples.chinese: 你每年的汽油费是多少？

examples.english: Falling sales have forced companies to cut costs.
examples.chinese: 销量下降迫使公司降低成本。

examples.english: I'm looking for a car with low running costs.
examples.chinese: 我在找一辆运行成本低的汽车。

examples.english: The best business is one with a small outlay and with no risk involved.
examples.chinese: 最好的企业是创建成本少、没有风险的企业。

examples.english: When we built the factory the outlay on machinery was heavy but we were able to buy all the latest equipment.
examples.chinese: 我们办工厂时买机器的花费很大，不过我们能买到最先进的设备。

examples.english: T- shirts are easy to produce, requiring little initial outlay and a minimum of time and effort.
examples.chinese: T恤衫容易生产，前期投入的费用少，所需的时间和精力也是最低限度的。

examples.english: The outgoings on a house this size are very high.
examples.chinese: 这样大的房子，开销非常大。

examples.english: I wrote a check for $200 to cover various outgoings.
examples.chinese: 我开了一张200美元的支票支付各种开销。

examples.english: The cost of the movie was high because of the large production overhead.
examples.chinese: 这部电影成本很高，因为摄制费用庞大。

examples.english: The company's overheads were much lower this year owing to the closure of several offices nationwide.
examples.chinese: 由于全国各地有几家事务所停业，今年公司的经营费用降低了很多。

examples.english: Restaurant prices have been put up to cover the ever-increasing overheads.
examples.chinese: 为支付越来越高的经营费用，餐厅都涨价了。

examples.english: John and Rachel have a new baby, so they have a lot of expenses right now.
examples.chinese: 约翰和雷切尔有了一个新生宝宝，眼下开销很大。

examples.english: Living expenses are much higher in London.
examples.chinese: 伦敦的生活费用要高很多。

examples.english: The company doesn't pay my travel expenses.
examples.chinese: 公司不支付我的旅费。

examples.english: You can choose any type of wood for your furniture, according to your budget.
examples.chinese: 你可以根据自己的预算选择任何一种木料做家具。

examples.english: Government cuts in the defence budget have meant a loss of 2000 jobs.
examples.chinese: 政府削减国防预算意味着将减少2,000个就业机会。

centers.english: to spend time

examples.english: I never seem to have any time to spend with the children.
examples.chinese: 我好像总是没有时间和孩子们在一起。

expressions.text: spend an hour/two days/a week etc

examples.english: Dani spends hours on the phone.
examples.chinese: 丹尼花上几小时打电话。

expressions.text: spend an hour/two days/a week etc doing something

examples.english: Fay spent a year in Italy teaching English.
examples.chinese: 费伊在意大利待了一年教英语。

examples.english: He spent the whole morning reading the report.
examples.chinese: 他整个上午都在看这个报告。

examples.english: I started doing a crossword to pass the time .
examples.chinese: 我开始做一个纵横字谜来打发时间。

examples.english: The security guards used to pass their time playing cards.
examples.chinese: 警卫过去常玩纸牌消磨时间。

expressions.text: kill time/a couple of hours etc

examples.english: I was early, so I sat in a café, killing time.
examples.chinese: 我来早了，就坐在一个咖啡馆里打发时间。

examples.english: The train doesn't leave till two, so we have a couple of hours to kill.
examples.chinese: 火车两点才开，所以我们还得等几个小时。

examples.english: Martin sat down nervously and busied himself rearranging the papers on his desk.
examples.chinese: 马丁紧张地坐下来，忙着整理桌上的文件。

examples.english: Mrs Smithers pottered about busying herself with light household tasks.
examples.chinese: 史密瑟斯夫人悠然地做一些轻便的家务活。

examples.english: You'll probably find Dave at the pool hall -- he often hangs out there.
examples.chinese: 你在桌球房可能会找到戴夫，他经常泡在那儿。

examples.english: I used to hang out with them when I was at college.
examples.chinese: 我上大学时经常和他们混在一起。

centers.english: to spend time working

examples.english: She usually ends up putting in several extra hours work at weekends.
examples.chinese: 她在周末通常还要多花好几个小时来工作。

examples.english: When I'm preparing for a tournament I put in thirty or forty hours of training a week.
examples.chinese: 我在准备锦标赛的时候，一个星期要花30到40个小时来训练。

examples.english: He's decided to give up racing and devote all his time to his farm in Ireland.
examples.chinese: 他已决定放弃比赛，把所有的时间都花在他爱尔兰的农场上。

examples.english: She intends to devote the next ten years to her charitable work.
examples.chinese: 她打算在今后的十年里致力于做慈善工作。

centers.english: to spend a period of time in a particular job or in prison
centers.chinese: 为某工作任职或服刑一段时间

examples.english: Reagan was serving his second term as President at the time.
examples.chinese: 里根当时正在他的第二届总统任期内。

examples.english: Smith has already served a ten-year sentence for armed robbery.
examples.chinese: 史密斯因持枪抢劫已经服刑十年。

examples.english: As a young teacher she did two years in one of the city's toughest schools.
examples.chinese: 作为一名青年教师，她在市里一所最严格的学校里教了两年书。

examples.english: I did my time in the army like everyone else.
examples.chinese: 我和其他人一样也服过役。

centers.english: to use your time badly

examples.english: Stop wasting time. We have to finish this today.
examples.chinese: 别浪费时间了，我们今天必须完成这事。

examples.english: I must have wasted two hours trying to fix my car.
examples.chinese: 我肯定已浪费了两小时的时间来修这台机器。

examples.english: Now she feels she's wasted her life.
examples.chinese: 有时候她觉得自己浪费生命。

examples.english: While you're talking, we're losing valuable time.
examples.chinese: 你们在讲话的时候，我们就浪费了宝贵的时间。

examples.english: The work is already behind schedule. The firm's lost at least 45 days through staff illness.
examples.chinese: 工作已经落后了，由于员工生病，公司至少损失了45天时间。

centers.english: what the value of something is

examples.english: The value of the sculpture was estimated at $500,000.
examples.chinese: 这尊雕塑的价值估计为50万美元。

examples.english: Some fine wines increase in value as they get older.
examples.chinese: 有些好酒越陈越值钱。

examples.english: How much is your ring worth?
examples.chinese: 你的戒指值多少钱?

examples.english: I guess their house must be worth about £500,000.
examples.chinese: 我猜他家的房子肯定值50万英镑。

examples.english: That old piano can't be worth more than $200.
examples.chinese: 那架旧钢琴最多值200美元。

examples.english: As a wedding present he gave her some pearls valued at $350,000.
examples.chinese: 他送给她一些价值为35万美元的珍珠作为结婚礼物。

examples.english: They have a classic car which has been valued at a higher price than they paid for it.
examples.chinese: 他们有一辆老式汽车，它的估价比他们买进时的要高。

examples.english: At current market prices their holding in the company is valued at over $25 million.
examples.chinese: 按目前的市场价，他们在这家公司拥有的股份估价为二千五百多万美元。

centers.english: worth a lot of money

examples.english: Don't lose this ring -- it's very valuable.
examples.chinese: 别把这枚戒指弄丢了，它很值钱。

examples.english: Besides her studio apartment, she also owns a valuable estate in Italy.
examples.chinese: 除了一套单室公寓，她还在意大利拥有一处值钱的地产。

examples.english: Rogers had purchased a valuable Hebrew manuscript from a dealer in Jerusalem.
examples.chinese: 罗杰斯从耶路撒冷的一位商人那里购得了贵重的希伯来文手稿。

examples.english: He has a few medals of great value.
examples.chinese: 他有几枚贵重的奖章。

examples.english: The burglars stole the television and video, but nothing of great value.
examples.chinese: 窃贼偷走了电视机和录像机，但没偷什么贵重东西。

examples.english: You should look after those old dolls - one day they could be worth a lot.
examples.chinese: 你要看好这些旧的玩具娃娃一有一天会值大钱的。

examples.english: He was very poor when he died, but now his paintings are worth a fortune.
examples.chinese: 他死的时候很穷，但是现在他的画值钱了。

examples.english: The robe was encrusted with precious metals and stones.
examples.chinese: 袍子上镶满了金银珠宝。

examples.english: The house was full of priceless antiques.
examples.chinese: 房子里全是稀世古董。

examples.english: a priceless oil painting
examples.chinese: 一幅贵重的油画

centers.english: valuable objects or possessions

examples.english: The hotel management advises guests to deposit their valuables in the hotel safe.
examples.chinese: 酒店管理人员建议客人把贵重物品存放在酒店保险箱内。

examples.english: Thieves will take any bags, wallets, and other valuables they can find.
examples.chinese: 窃贼们会拿走任何能找到的提包、钱包和其他贵重物品。

examples.english: The exhibition shows treasures from ancient China.
examples.chinese: 展览会展示中国古代的艺术珍品。

examples.english: Some of Spain's most valuable art treasures are housed in El Prado.
examples.chinese: 西班牙一些最有价值的艺术珍品被收藏在埃尔普拉多博物馆里。

centers.english: to decide what the value of something is

examples.english: He works for an antique firm, valuing furniture.
examples.chinese: 他在一家古董行工作，给家具估价。

examples.english: The apartment was appraised, and Stephen gave Julie a check for half the amount.
examples.chinese: 这套公寓估好价之后，斯蒂芬给朱莉开了一张支票给她一半的钱。

examples.english: The builder valued the work already done at $400.
examples.chinese: 建筑商给已完成部分作价400美元。

examples.english: Those silver bowls could be worth a lot of money -- you ought to have them appraised.
examples.chinese: 那些银碗可能很值钱一你应该请人估估价。

examples.english: I took the ring to a shop for valuation.
examples.chinese: 我把戒指拿到一家店里去估价。

examples.english: The valuer person whose job is to value things said the plate was worth £500.
examples.chinese: 估价师说这只盘子值500英镑。

centers.english: to become more valuable

examples.english: They bought a Ferrari knowing that it would increase in value.
examples.chinese: 他们买了一辆法拉利车，因为知道它会升值。

examples.english: Through clever marketing, the shares have gone up in value quite quickly.
examples.chinese: 通过精明的市场策略，这些股票很快升值了。

examples.english: The pound has risen in value against the yen over the weekend.
examples.chinese: 周末期间，英镑兑日元的汇率已经上升了。

examples.english: This property has appreciated rapidly during the last ten years.
examples.chinese: 在过去的十年中，该房产升值很快。

examples.english: Most investments are still expected to appreciate at a steady rate.
examples.chinese: 大多数投资仍有望稳步增值。

examples.english: Fortunately, when I sold my apartment it had almost doubled in value since I bought it.
examples.chinese: 幸运的是，我把公寓卖掉的时候，它的价值几乎已经是我购买时的两倍。

examples.english: Production of iron and steel more than tripled in value during the 1950s.
examples.chinese: 20世纪50年代，钢铁的产量增长了两倍多。

centers.english: to become less valuable

examples.english: Gold and silver have gone down in value.
examples.chinese: 黄金和白银贬值了。

examples.english: Most European currencies fell in value yesterday.
examples.chinese: 昨天大多数欧洲货币都下跌了。

examples.english: A new car depreciates more quickly than a second-hand one.
examples.chinese: 新车贬值比二手车贬值快。

examples.english: US investors anticipate that the Deutschmark will, in the long term, depreciate relative to the dollar.
examples.chinese: 美国投资者预计从长远看，德国马克兑美元会贬值。

examples.english: The price of the equipment must reflect depreciation.
examples.chinese: 设备价格必须反映折旧因素。

centers.english: without any value

examples.english: When he died, all my uncle left me was a worthless plot of land.
examples.chinese: 叔叔去世时只留给我一块不值钱的土地。

examples.english: The jewellery turned out to be completely worthless.
examples.chinese: 这些珠宝最终变得一文不值。

examples.english: I'm afraid this banknote is a forgery; it's just a worthless piece of paper.
examples.chinese: 恐怕这张钞票是假的，这只是一张不值钱的纸。

examples.english: I don't think my stereo is worth anything now, but it was quite expensive when I bought it.
examples.chinese: 我想现在我的立体声设备一文不值了，但我买的时候是很贵的。

centers.english: to do things that need doing

examples.english: Who is dealing with the accommodation arrangements for the conference?
examples.chinese: 是谁负责安排这次会议的住宿？

examples.english: I spend most of my working day dealing with customer inquiries.
examples.chinese: 我工作日的大多数时候都在处理顾客的询问。

examples.english: I'm sorry I'm late. I had an urgent call to deal with.
examples.chinese: 很抱歉我迟到了。我接了一个紧急电话。

examples.english: I'll join you later - there are a few things I need to see to at the office first.
examples.chinese: 我稍后才和你会合。办公室里有几件事需要先处理。

examples.english: My brother attended to all the funeral arrangements.
examples.chinese: 我哥哥安排了葬礼的一切事宜。

examples.english: You'd better get someone to see to that leaking pipe.
examples.chinese: 你最好找个人来修理一下那渗漏的管子。

examples.english: I'll see to it that everything is ready on the day.
examples.chinese: 我会确保处理好那一天的一切事情。

examples.english: My secretary will take care of the details.
examples.chinese: 我的秘书会处理所有细节的。

examples.english: Shall I take care of your mail for you while you are away?
examples.chinese: 你不在时由我来处理你的信件好吗？

examples.english: Don't worry about your passport and visa - it's all taken care of.
examples.chinese: 别担心你的护照和签证，一切都为你办妥了。

examples.english: ‘We need to make sure the others know where we'll be meeting.’ ‘Leave it to me. I'll phone them when I get home.’
examples.chinese: “我们要确保其他人知道我们要在哪里开会。”“这事交给我吧。回家后我会打电话给他们。”

examples.english: You should allow two weeks for your visa application to be processed.
examples.chinese: 你的签证申请要两周的时间处理。

examples.english: Computers have given banks the power to process millions of transactions a day.
examples.chinese: 有了电脑，银行一天能处理数百万笔交易。

examples.english: I got your e-mail but I'm afraid I forgot to follow it up.
examples.chinese: 我收到了你的电子邮件，但却忘了跟进那件事。

examples.english: Our rule is that the complaints department must follow up a letter within two days.
examples.chinese: 我们规定投诉受理部门必须在两天内对来信作出处理。

examples.english: Following up on recommendations made last year, the president called for the more efficient use of resources.
examples.chinese: 作为对去年的提议的回应，总统呼吁要更有效地使用各种资源。

centers.english: to deal with a problem or difficult situation
centers.chinese: 处理问题或困难的局面

examples.english: Many schools are now trying to tackle the problem of drug abuse.
examples.chinese: 许多学校现正设法解决滥用药物的问题。

examples.english: The new laws are aimed at tackling unemployment.
examples.chinese: 新法律旨在解决失业问题。

examples.english: Police forces in the area are trying to tackle car crime head on.
examples.chinese: 该地区警方正设法迎头打击汽车犯罪活动。

examples.english: There were a few problems, but nothing I couldn't handle.
examples.chinese: 出了些问题，但没有我应付不了的事。

examples.english: A lot of people find it difficult to handle criticism.
examples.chinese: 许多人觉得难以处理批评。

examples.english: The whole situation has been very badly handled.
examples.chinese: 对整个局面的处理手法非常糟糕。

examples.english: It's her first year as a doctor, but she is handling the pressures of the job very well.
examples.chinese: 这是她当医生的第一年，可她对工作压力应付得很好。

examples.english: I spent the weekend sorting out my tax affairs.
examples.chinese: 我花了整个周末处理我的税务问题。

examples.english: We'll have to sort your immigration status out before we can offer you a job.
examples.chinese: 我们得先解决你的移民身份问题，然后才能为你提供工作。

examples.english: I decided to take a week's holiday to try and sort myself out.
examples.chinese: 我决定去度假一周，尽量去解决自己的个人问题。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: The authorities have been grappling with the problem for a decade, but cars still choke the streets in the rush hour.
examples.chinese: 当局十年来一直在努力解决该问题，但在高峰时间汽车仍堵塞街道。

examples.english: There is no environmental policy in a country that is still grappling with increasing poverty.
examples.chinese: 一个仍然在努力解决日益严重的贫穷问题的国家是不会有环境政策的。

expressions.text: take the bull by the horns

examples.english: Helena decided to take the bull by the horns and organize the show herself.
examples.chinese: 海伦娜决心果敢地解决这一问题，由自己来组织这场演出。

centers.english: to deal successfully with a difficult situation

examples.english: ‘How did you manage while you were unemployed?’ ‘Luckily, I had some savings.’
examples.chinese: “你失业的时候是怎么渡过来的？”“所幸的是我有一些积蓄。”

examples.english: I'll be away for a week, do you think you can manage on your own?
examples.chinese: 我要离开一个星期，你觉得你一个人可以应付吗?

examples.english: Helen was always a difficult child. None of her teachers knew how to manage her.
examples.chinese: 海伦这孩子总是很难对付，她的老师中没有一个知道怎样才能管住她。

examples.english: The seminar discusses typical work-related problems and strategies to manage them.
examples.chinese: 研讨会的议题是工作上的典型问题以及处理这些问题的策略。

examples.english: It's a tough job but I'm sure he'll cope.
examples.chinese: 这工作很棘手，但我肯定他能应付。

examples.english: When I got back from holiday, I had an enormous backlog of work to cope with.
examples.chinese: 我度假回来后有大量积压的工作要处理。

examples.english: The kids were very young and it was difficult to cope financially.
examples.chinese: 这些孩子还小，经济上也不容易应付。

examples.english: People who cope successfully with difficult situations usually look ahead and anticipate the circumstances.
examples.chinese: 能成功应付困境的人一般都会向前看，预计未来情况。

examples.english: Her friends helped her to get through the first awful weeks after Bill died.
examples.chinese: 比尔去世后，是朋友们帮助她捱过了头几个星期的可怕日子的。

expressions.gram: verb phrase not in progressive or passive

examples.english: The police have the situation under control.
examples.chinese: 警方控制住了局面。

examples.english: The flight was very bumpy but the pilot assured us that everything was under control.
examples.chinese: 飞机颠得厉害，但机长向我们保证一切都在控制之中。

examples.english: The disease is spreading so fast that it is going to take years to bring it under control.
examples.chinese: 这一疾病传播得如此迅速，要控制住它需要许多年时间。

examples.english: During the 1990s low oil prices helped to keep inflation under control.
examples.chinese: 20世纪90年代的低油价对控制通货膨胀起到了作用。

examples.english: I've always wanted to overcome my fear of spiders.
examples.chinese: 我一直想克服对蜘蛛的恐惧。

examples.english: It's perfectly normal to be a bit nervous. I'm sure you'll get over it once you start your presentation.
examples.chinese: 有点胆怯是很正常的。我相信你开始作报告后就会克服过去的。

examples.english: The school overcame the problem of funding by getting local firms to sponsor them.
examples.chinese: 该校得到本地公司的赞助，解决了经费问题。

examples.english: The residents of the small town are still struggling to come to grips with the tragedy.
examples.chinese: 小镇的居民至今仍在努力使自己面对这场悲剧。

examples.english: Teachers must be prepared to spend time getting to grips with new technology.
examples.chinese: 教师们必须准备花时间去了解新的技术。

examples.english: No country has really got to grips with the problem of nuclear waste.
examples.chinese: 没有一个国家真正认真地解决过核废料的问题。

examples.english: We are calling on all our employees to rise to the occasion and become more efficient and productive.
examples.chinese: 我们号召全体员工全力应对困境，以更高的效率和生产力来完成工作。

examples.english: The team rose to the challenge and fought back to produce another goal.
examples.chinese: 该球队全力应战，奋起反击并再次攻入一球。

expressions.text: rise to the challenge of something

examples.english: Naylor was one of those men who rise to the challenge of danger.
examples.chinese: 内勒是那种敢于挺身挑战危险的人。

examples.english: Liz seems to be taking the divorce in her stride.
examples.chinese: 莉兹好像对离婚的事处之泰然。

examples.english: Most kids get teased a bit at school - they have to learn to take it in their stride.
examples.chinese: 大多数孩子在学校都会受到一些取笑，他们得学会从容地应对。

examples.english: Nigel smiled and took the criticism in his stride.
examples.chinese: 奈杰尔笑了笑，对批评处之泰然。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: Kate rose above all the trouble at home and did well in her classes.
examples.chinese: 凯特摆脱了家中所有的烦恼，在课堂上表现很好。

examples.english: Immigrants to the country were struggling to survive and rise above the poverty that surrounded them.
examples.chinese: 移民在这个国家里奋力求存，竭力要摆脱困扰他们的贫穷问题。

centers.english: to deal with a difficult situation in a particular way

examples.english: The school are treating this matter very seriously.
examples.chinese: 校方以十分严肃的态度对待此事。

examples.english: No one would suggest that sex offenders should be treated lightly.
examples.chinese: 没有人会提出对性侵犯者从轻发落。

examples.english: The company treats training as a continuous part of career development.
examples.chinese: 这家公司把培训看作是事业持续发展的一个部分。

examples.english: Police say that her death is being treated as suspicious.
examples.chinese: 警方表示她的死被认为有可疑之处。

examples.english: Have you decided how you want to play it?
examples.chinese: 你决定了如何处理这事吗？

examples.english: You can get exactly what you want if you play it carefully.
examples.chinese: 如果你小心处理好此事，你就能完全得到想要的结果。

examples.english: In the run-up to the election, politicians in both parties just wanted to play it safe.
examples.chinese: 在大选前的准备阶段，双方政客都只想稳妥行事。

examples.english: Some of the people involved in the competition, were not playing it straight.
examples.chinese: 这次比赛中有一些人没有遵守公平的原则。

examples.english: ‘Play straight with me or I'll kill you,’ he hissed.
examples.chinese: “别跟我耍花招，否则我就要你的命。”他嘘声道。

examples.english: She would not show him how upset she was. It was always smarter to play it cool.
examples.chinese: 她不会对他表示她有多不开心。沉着一点总是较为聪明。

examples.english: ‘What'll you do if he asks you?’ ‘I'm not sure, I'll play it by ear.’
examples.chinese: “他要是问你该怎么办呢？”“我也不知道，到时候见机行事吧。”

examples.english: I don't think refusing to negotiate is the right way to approach this problem.
examples.chinese: 我觉得拒绝谈判不是着手处理这个问题的正确办法。

examples.english: Try to relax before the exam, and you'll approach it in a better frame of mind.
examples.chinese: 考试前尽量放松一下，这样考试时精神状态就会好些。

centers.english: when there are difficult problems that you must deal with
centers.chinese: 遇到必须处理的难题时

examples.english: Latin America faces a growing debt problem.
examples.chinese: 拉丁美洲正面临着日趋严重的债务问题。

examples.english: McManus knew he was facing the biggest challenge of his career.
examples.chinese: 麦克马纳斯知道自己面临着事业上最大的挑战。

examples.english: This report highlights some of the problems faced by learners of English.
examples.chinese: 这份报告突显了英语学习者面对的一些问题。

examples.english: The new administration faces the difficult task of rebuilding the country's economy.
examples.chinese: 新政府面临着重建国家经济的艰巨任务。

examples.english: He was faced with the task of breaking the bad news to the boy's relatives.
examples.chinese: 他面临着要把这坏消息转告男孩亲属的任务。

examples.english: Today's violence highlights the problems faced by the government here.
examples.chinese: 今天的暴行突显了这里的政府所面对的种种问题。

examples.english: Nurses are confronted with life-or-death situations on a daily basis.
examples.chinese: 护士每天都要面对生死攸关的各种情况。

examples.english: He remembered when he was first confronted by the racial realities of living in South Africa.
examples.chinese: 他想起了居住在南非时第一次遇到种族歧视现实的情景。

examples.english: The chairman also had to contend with divisions among the committee members.
examples.chinese: 主席还要去解决委员会成员之间的不和。

examples.english: Rescuers were having to contend with cold weather, snow and ice.
examples.chinese: 营救人员必须应付寒冷的天气、雪和冰这些恶劣条件。

examples.english: The Principal accused parents of not facing up to their responsibilities.
examples.chinese: 校长指责家长们不敢面对自己的责任。

examples.english: It's only by facing up to her addiction that she can hope to live a normal life again.
examples.chinese: 她只有勇敢地正视自己的毒瘾问题，才有希望重新过上正常的生活。

examples.english: It's time the government faced up to spending more on health and education.
examples.chinese: 政府该正视在保健与教育领域增加开支这个问题了。

examples.english: You should face up to things, not just pretend that nothing's happening.
examples.chinese: 你要敢于面对发生的事情，而不是假装什么事都没发生。

examples.english: You'll have the boss to reckon with if you go home this early.
examples.chinese: 如果你这么早回家，老板会找你麻烦的。

examples.english: Anyone attempting to invade the country will have to reckon with the peacekeeping force.
examples.chinese: 谁若企图侵略该国必须先面对维和部队。

centers.english: a particular way of dealing with something

examples.english: The best course of action would be to speak to her and tell her the whole story.
examples.chinese: 最好的办法是跟她谈谈，把整件事都告诉她。

examples.english: One possible course of action is to increase taxes on alcohol and tobacco.
examples.chinese: 一个可行的办法是提高烟酒税。

examples.english: Jim didn't want to start an argument, so agreeing seemed to be the safest course.
examples.chinese: 吉姆不想挑起争论，所以赞同似乎是最安全的做法。

examples.english: The company needs to adopt a much more radical approach.
examples.chinese: 这家公司必须采取更彻底的解决办法。

examples.english: Each of the delegates suggested a different approach to the problem.
examples.chinese: 各个代表对这个问题都提出了不同的处理方法。

examples.english: the government's aggressive approach to the question of homelessness
examples.chinese: 政府对无家可归这个问题所采取的积极的解决办法

examples.english: Working full-time may not be your best option.
examples.chinese: 全职工作不一定是你的最佳选择。

examples.english: What other options do I have?
examples.chinese: 我还有什么选择？

examples.english: Our only option now is to contact the police.
examples.chinese: 我们现在唯一的选择是跟警方联系。

examples.english: These people have no option but to take low-paid unattractive work.
examples.chinese: 这些人除了接受低薪乏味的工作外，别无选择。

examples.english: As for replacement fuels, many people do not like to contemplate the nuclear option.
examples.chinese: 说到替代燃料，许多人都不愿意考虑把核燃料作为选择方案。

examples.english: For getting around the city centre, a bicycle's your best bet.
examples.chinese: 在市中心逛逛的话，骑一辆自行车是最佳选择。

expressions.text: somebody's best bet is to do something

examples.english: We decided that our best bet was to leave him where he was and go and get help.
examples.chinese: 我们决定最好的办法是把他留在原地去找人来帮忙。

centers.english: the work that companies do

examples.english: Business in Europe has been badly affected by economic conditions in Asia.
examples.chinese: 欧洲的业务受到亚洲经济局势的不良影响。

examples.english: She'll be back next week - she's in Korea on business.
examples.chinese: 她下周回来—她去日本出差了。

examples.english: He's been in the advertising business for over 20 years now, and he wants to get out.
examples.chinese: 他从事广告业至今已二十多年，他想不干这一行了。

examples.english: Spending a year working for a big company will be good for him - at the moment he has no head for business at all.
examples.chinese: 在大公司工作一年对他有好处——现在他一点商业头脑都没有。

examples.english: The introduction of the Euro should make trade between European countries much easier.
examples.chinese: 欧元的使用应该能给欧洲各国间的贸易带来更多便利。

examples.english: The trade in data processing between countries is likely to grow faster than the trade in goods.
examples.chinese: 各国间的数据处理贸易很可能比商品贸易增长得更快。

examples.english: South Korea and Japan have signed an important trade agreement.
examples.chinese: 韩国与日本签订了一份重要的贸易协定。

examples.english: The trade deficit with China remains high.
examples.chinese: 与中国的贸易逆差仍然很高。

examples.english: The U.S. has maintained a trade embargo against Cuba since 1962.
examples.chinese: 美国从1962年起开始对古巴实施贸易禁运。

examples.english: Strong exports of services helped the overall balance of trade.
examples.chinese: 强劲的服务输出有助于改善总体的贸易差额。

examples.english: After agriculture, overseas trade accounts for the largest portion of the economy.
examples.chinese: 海外贸易排在农业之后占了经济的第二大份额。

examples.english: The war has created favorable conditions for the illegal arms trade.
examples.chinese: 这场战争为非法军火贸易创造了有利条件。

examples.english: One of the roles of the federal government is to regulate interstate commerce.
examples.chinese: 联邦政府的职责之一是管理州际贸易。

examples.english: He had a genuine talent for commerce and soon had a brilliant career working for the World Bank.
examples.chinese: 他具有经商的天赋，在世界银行的工作没用多久就极为成功。

examples.english: The region has tried to attract new industry in order to reduce unemployment.
examples.chinese: 该地区已设法吸引新的工业以降低失业率。

examples.english: Many people moved from Asia to work in the British textile industry, where jobs were plentiful.
examples.chinese: 许多人从亚洲移民到英国从事纺织业，那里有大量的就业机会。

examples.english: She was looking for a management position in industry.
examples.chinese: 她想在制造业寻找一份管理工作。

examples.english: The Ruhr valley has always been the centre of German heavy industry.
examples.chinese: 鲁尔谷一向是德国重工业中心。

examples.english: Ireland is now a European center for light industry, like computer assembly.
examples.chinese: 爱尔兰现已成为欧洲的轻工业中心，譬如说电脑装配。

examples.english: E-commerce is still a small but fast-growing part of the U.S. economy.
examples.chinese: 电子商务仍是美国经济中规模较小但增长迅速的部分。

examples.english: a conference to debate the future of e-commerce
examples.chinese: 探讨电子商务的未来的会议

examples.english: Salco may have to close down its UK operations with the loss of 1500 jobs.
examples.chinese: 萨尔科公司可能被迫停止在英国的业务，这将使1,500人失业。

examples.english: He was an important decision maker in terms of GM's overseas operations.
examples.chinese: 他是通用汽车公司海外业务的重要决策者。

examples.english: The company had to pay a lot of tax on its financial dealings during the past tax year.
examples.chinese: 该公司要为其上一个税务年度内所进行的金融交易付大量的税款。

examples.english: Mr Stockwell's dealings with several Third World banks are currently under investigation.
examples.chinese: 斯托克韦尔先生与几家第三世界银行所进行的交易目前正在接受调查。

examples.english: The group is planning to risk everything to get their next venture off the ground.
examples.chinese: 为了使下一个风险项目顺利开展，该集团打算把公司的一切都押作赌注。

examples.english: His bankruptcy was the result of several reckless business ventures.
examples.chinese: 他的破产是由几次鲁莽的商业冒险活动所致。

examples.english: Ford has invested $125 million in a joint venture to build engines in China.
examples.chinese: 福特公司在一个合营项目中投资了1.25亿美元在中国生产汽车发动机。

centers.english: the amount of business a company is doing

examples.english: Business is really bad at the moment. They may have to sell some of their factories overseas.
examples.chinese: 目前的业务情况很糟糕。也许他们得卖掉一些在海外的工厂。

examples.english: Business was good until June and then sales fell because people were on vacation.
examples.chinese: 营业额到6月份仍表现良好，但此后由于人们出外度假，销量便下降了。

examples.english: In the old days, when business was booming, he used to fly to New York twice a week.
examples.chinese: 以前当生意兴隆时，他一周要乘飞机去纽约两次。

examples.english: Building the new highway will be good for business.
examples.chinese: 建造这条新公路将为贸易带来好处。

examples.english: Our corporation has an annual turnover of $3.2 billion.
examples.chinese: 我们公司每年的营业额达32亿美元。

examples.english: Turnover is expected to double now that the recession is over.
examples.chinese: 经济衰退期已过，估计营业额将翻一番。

examples.english: Sales have been far better than expected.
examples.chinese: 销售额比预期的要好得多。

examples.english: These firms report sales of between 10 and 20 million dollars a year.
examples.chinese: 这些公司表示每年的销售额在1，000万到2,000万元之间。

examples.english: December sales figures will be released on Thursday.
examples.chinese: 12月份的销售数字将于星期四公布。

centers.english: relating to business

examples.english: During the first week of the secretarial course we learned how to write business letters.
examples.chinese: 上秘书课的第一周，我们学会了如何写商业信函。

examples.english: Most of the women there were wearing business suits.
examples.chinese: 那里的大部分女士都穿职业套装。

examples.english: He's in Tokyo on a business trip.
examples.chinese: 他出差到东京去了。

examples.english: I've known Mr Henry for years. He's one of my father's old business associates.
examples.chinese: 我认识亨利先生许多年了。他是我父亲在生意上的一个老伙伴。

examples.english: His first commercial venture was opening a small corner shop.
examples.chinese: 他的第一次商业冒险就是在街角开了个小店。

examples.english: The British Empire was established for commercial as well as political reasons.
examples.chinese: 大英帝国是基于商业及政治理由而建立的。

examples.english: The space shuttle is being used more and more for commercial purposes.
examples.chinese: 航天飞机被越来越多地用于商业目的。

examples.english: Commercially, the movie was a disaster.
examples.chinese: 从商业的角度看，这部影片是彻底失败的。

examples.english: The government is giving high priority to industrial development.
examples.chinese: 该政府视工业发展为重点项目。

examples.english: industrial waste
examples.chinese: 工业废料

centers.english: to do business

examples.english: I hope you'll think about my offer. I'm sure we can do business.
examples.chinese: 希望你能考虑一下我方的报价。我相信我们能做成这笔生意。

examples.english: They do a lot of business with Italian companies.
examples.chinese: 他们与意大利公司有大量生意往来。

examples.english: She's very efficient - the kind of person you want to do business with.
examples.chinese: 她办事很有效率—是那种你愿意与之做生意的人。

examples.english: They've been doing very good business lately. They'll probably even expand their operations.
examples.chinese: 最近他们的生意一直非常好。他们甚至有可能扩大业务。

examples.english: In all the twenty years I've been in business this is the worst period I've seen for sales.
examples.chinese: 我做了20年的生意，这是我所经历过的最糟糕的销售期。

examples.english: The insurance companies are in business to make money, not waste it.
examples.chinese: 保险公司做生意是为了赚钱而非浪费金钱。

examples.english: When I qualified I set up in business as a financial consultant.
examples.chinese: 我取得资格之后开办了一家金融咨询公司。

examples.english: I deal with farmers, selling them things like cattle feed and insecticides.
examples.chinese: 我与农场主做生意，向他们出售牛饲料及杀虫剂等东西。

examples.english: They don't buy their office supplies from a store - they only deal with the manufacturers.
examples.chinese: 他们不是从商店里买办公室用品的—他们只向生产商订购。

examples.english: We don't deal with the actors directly - we usually have to go through their agents.
examples.chinese: 我们不直接与演员们接触—通常要通过他们的经纪人。

examples.english: Companies that deal in oil should prepare themselves for a price drop.
examples.chinese: 做石油生意的公司应对价格下跌及早做好准备。

examples.english: The main commodities he dealt in were rice, and lentils.
examples.chinese: 他经营的商品主要是大米和小扁豆。

examples.english: The two nations have not traded with each other for over 30 years.
examples.chinese: 这两个国家已有三十多年没有贸易往来了。

examples.english: Japan is one of our major trading partners.
examples.chinese: 日本是我们的主要贸易伙伴之一。

examples.english: Olivetti operates in all the major computer markets in the world.
examples.chinese: 奥利维提公司在全世界所有主要的电脑市场上都有业务。

examples.english: Screenview are a small company operating from a converted barn in a village near Norwich.
examples.chinese: 斯克林维是一家小公司，他们在诺里奇附近的村庄内一个经过改建的仓库内经营。

centers.english: a business agreement

examples.english: Wickes lost a lot of money on two large property deals.
examples.chinese: 威克斯在两大宗房地产交易中赔了许多钱。

examples.english: They agreed a $55 million deal with a leading Japanese automobile company.
examples.chinese: 他们同意与一家主要的日本汽车公司做一笔5，500万美元的生意。

examples.english: Taylor recently signed a deal to lease her three-bedroom home for $14,000.
examples.chinese: 泰勒最近签了一份租约，把她有三个卧室的住房以14,000美元的价钱出租了。

examples.english: It is expected that the deal will be finalized before the end of May.
examples.chinese: 估计这笔交易将于5月底之前完成。

examples.english: When the transaction is complete it will be at least two weeks before you receive your copy of the contract.
examples.chinese: 这笔交易完成后，至少再过两个星期你才能收到合同副本。

examples.english: Most transactions are processed by computer at our Head Office.
examples.chinese: 大部分交易都是由我们总部的电脑处理的。

centers.english: someone who works in business

examples.english: Tim Knight is a high-powered businessman who runs his own electronics company.
examples.chinese: 蒂姆·奈特是个干劲十足的商人，管理着自己的一家电子公司。

examples.english: A successful businesswoman, she had made her first million before she was 21.
examples.chinese: 她是个成功的商人，不到21岁就挣了她的第一个一百万。

examples.english: The Bay Area is full of entrepreneurs hoping to make money on the Internet.
examples.chinese: 海湾区域有许多满怀希望要靠互联网发财的创业者。

examples.english: A few months ago a young property entrepreneur bought a vacant house, redecorated it and sold it for twice the original value.
examples.chinese: 几个月前，一位年轻的房地产商人买下一幢空房子，重新装修之后便以原价两倍的价钱卖出。

centers.english: to let someone do something

examples.english: We wanted to go camping, but our parents wouldn't let us.
examples.chinese: 我们想去野营，但父母不允许。

examples.english: Sue doesn't let her kids eat candy.
examples.chinese: 休不让她的孩子吃糖。

examples.english: Thanks for letting me spend the night at your place.
examples.chinese: 谢谢你让我在你家度过了这个晚上。

examples.english: You'd better let the dog out.
examples.chinese: 你最好让那狗出去。

examples.english: We do not allow people to smoke anywhere in the building.
examples.chinese: 大楼里任何地方我们都不允许抽烟。！

examples.english: Under federal law, Indian nations are allowed to operate casinos on their reservations, with the state's permission.
examples.chinese: 根据联邦法律，印第安民族经政府许可，可以在他们的居留地经营赌场。

examples.english: Paul's bank now allows him £35 a week, and Geoff can withdraw no more than £40.
examples.chinese: 保罗的银行现在允许他一周提取35英镑，杰夫可以提取不超过40英镑。

examples.english: The manager doesn't allow children in the bar.
examples.chinese: 经理不允许小孩子进酒吧。

examples.english: Mom says we can each have two cookies.
examples.chinese: 妈妈说我们每个人都可以吃两块小甜饼。

examples.english: I thought you said we could use this room.
examples.chinese: 我记得你说过我们能借用这房间。

examples.english: The rebels finally agreed to a ban on terrorist activity.
examples.chinese: 叛乱分子终于同意禁止恐怖活动。

examples.english: McCaskill left the California Angels on Saturday and agreed to a three-year contract with the Chicago White Sox worth over $6 million.
examples.chinese: 麦卡斯基尔周六离开了加利福尼亚天使队，同意与芝加哥白袜队签约三年，报酬为六百多万美元。

examples.english: Photography will not be permitted inside the courtroom.
examples.chinese: 法庭内不准拍照。

examples.english: The law permits foreign investors to own up to 25% of British companies.
examples.chinese: 法律允许外国投资者最多可以拥有英国公司25%的股份。

examples.english: The water contained six times the permitted level of chlorine.
examples.chinese: 水中含有的氯是允许含量的六倍。

examples.english: Who authorized the decision to close the factory?
examples.chinese: 是谁批准关闭工厂的决定的?

examples.english: Among other matters, the city council authorized more funds for additional police officers.
examples.chinese: 除了别的一些事情，市议会还批准了更多的资金用于增聘警员。

examples.english: Only Congress can authorize the President to declare war.
examples.chinese: 只有国会才能授权总统宣战。

examples.english: As long as your parents have no objection, you're very welcome to come on vacation with us.
examples.chinese: 只要你父母不反对，我们非常欢迎你和我们一起去度假。

expressions.text: have no objection to somebody doing something

examples.english: They said they had no objection to us leaving a little early.
examples.chinese: 他们说不反对我们早走一会儿。

examples.english: Parents are required to give their written consent give permission in writing before a child can be asked to participate in an interview.
examples.chinese: 家长必须作出书面同意，才可以请小孩子参加采访。

examples.english: In September, he gave Dr. Arning his consent to conduct the experiment.
examples.chinese: 9月份，他同意阿宁博士做这个实验。

expressions.text: give somebody/something the go-ahead/give somebody/something the green light

examples.english: We're still waiting for the finance committee to give us the green light.
examples.chinese: 我们仍在等待财务委员会给我们开绿灯。

examples.english: Councillors in Darlington gave the hotel development project the go-ahead in September.
examples.chinese: 达灵顿的市政委员于9月份给这个酒店开发项目放行。

examples.english: We believe the nuclear programme should be given the go-ahead for further development.
examples.chinese: 我们相信，这个核计划应该能得到放行，可以继续开发。

centers.english: what you say to ask permission

examples.english: Can I borrow your pen for a minute?
examples.chinese: 我能借你的钢笔用一会儿吗？

examples.english: Hey Dad, can I stay at Sara's house tonight?
examples.chinese: 嗨，爸爸，我今天晚上可以住在萨拉家里吗?

examples.english: Can I go to the bathroom?
examples.chinese: 我可以去一下洗手间吗？

examples.english: May I ask you a question, Mr Simmonds?
examples.chinese: 我可以问您个问题吗，西蒙斯先生?

examples.english: May I see your ticket, please?
examples.chinese: 我可以看看您的票吗，先生？

expressions.text: do you mind if/would you mind if/is it all right if

examples.english: Is it all right if I smoke?
examples.chinese: 我可以抽烟吗?

examples.english: Do you mind if I open the window?
examples.chinese: 你介意我把窗户打开吗？

examples.english: Would you mind if I made a call?
examples.chinese: 我打个电话可以吗？

centers.english: what you say to give permission

examples.english: ‘Can I watch TV?’ ‘Sure, go ahead.’
examples.chinese: “我可以看电视吗？”“当然可以，去吧。”

examples.english: ‘Is it OK if I eat the last apple?’ ‘Go right ahead.’
examples.chinese: “我把最后一个苹果吃了好吗？”“吃吧。”

examples.english: If you want to take a shower, just go ahead and take one.
examples.chinese: 如果你想洗个淋浴，就去洗一个吧。

examples.english: ‘Is it all right if I call my parents?’ ‘Be my guest.’
examples.chinese: “我可以打电话给我父母吗？”“请便。”

examples.english: ‘Do you mind if I sit here?’ ‘Be my guest.’
examples.chinese: “你介意我坐在这儿吗？”“请便。”

examples.english: ‘I hope you don't mind if I use your phone.’ ‘Of course not. Feel free.’
examples.chinese: “我希望你不介意我用一下你的电话。”“当然可以，请随便。”

examples.english: Please feel free to stop me and ask questions whenever you like.
examples.chinese: 有问题请随时问我。

examples.english: ‘Is there any beer left?’ ‘Sure, help yourself.’
examples.chinese: “还有啤酒吗？”“有，自己来吧。”

examples.english: Help yourself to coffee and cake.
examples.chinese: 随便享用咖啡和蛋糕吧。

examples.english: ‘Can I take some more casserole?’ ‘Of course!’
examples.chinese: “我可以再来点砂锅菜吗？”“当然！”

examples.english: ‘Do you mind of I sit here?’ ‘Of course you can.’
examples.chinese: “我可以坐在这里吗？”“当然可以。

centers.english: to be allowed to do something

examples.english: Now that you're seventeen, you can learn to drive.
examples.chinese: 你已17岁了，可以学开车了。

examples.english: You can't park here.
examples.chinese: 你不能在这儿停车。

examples.english: Ask Jan if you can borrow a cup or two of milk.
examples.chinese: 问问简可不可以借一两杯牛奶。

examples.english: Are we allowed to use calculators on the test?
examples.chinese: 我们考试时可以使用计算器吗？

examples.english: Lanterns and heaters are allowed on Forest Service lands if they are fueled by propane.
examples.chinese: 提灯和取暖器若使用丙烷作为燃料，就可以在林务局所辖的林区里使用。

examples.english: Several international relief organizations had been allowed into the area to determine what help was needed.
examples.chinese: 几个国际救援组织已获准进入该地区，以评估需要哪些帮助。

examples.english: Residents were not even permitted to fish in the reservoir.
examples.chinese: 居民甚至不准在水库内捕鱼。

examples.english: Smoking is only permitted in the public lounge.
examples.chinese: 只有公共休息室里才允许抽烟。

examples.english: Educational institutions may videotape copyrighted television programs, but the tape must be destroyed after 45 days.
examples.chinese: 教育机构可以录下受版权保护的电视节目，但是45天之后必须销毁录像带。

examples.english: Workers are free to choose from a wide variety of insurance plans.
examples.chinese: 工人可以从多种保险计划中随意选择。

examples.english: The winners of the competition are free to spend their prize money in any way they choose.
examples.chinese: 比赛获胜者可以随意使用他们的奖金。

centers.english: to let something happen, without trying to stop it
centers.chinese: 允许某事发生而不去阻止

examples.english: You shouldn't let your husband treat you like that.
examples.chinese: 你不应该任由你丈夫那样对待你。

examples.english: Don't let the dog get into the flowerbed.
examples.chinese: 不要让狗进入花坛。

examples.english: It took all my willpower to remain cool and tell him not to let it happen again.
examples.chinese: 我竭力保持镇静，叫他不要再让这种事情发生。

examples.english: The government has allowed the present economic crisis to get completely out of control.
examples.chinese: 政府任由目前的经济危机完全失去控制。

examples.english: The hedge should not be allowed to grow higher than six feet.
examples.chinese: 树篱不应长得高过6英尺。

examples.english: If you really want to become a lawyer, I'm not going to stand in your way.
examples.chinese: 如果你真的想当律师，我不会阻拦你的。

examples.english: Sally knew that modelling would be her career, and she was going to make sure that nothing stood in her way.
examples.chinese: 萨莉知道当模特儿就是她的事业，她要确保没有任何事情妨碍她。

examples.english: A fire broke out in the warehouse and raged unchecked for over two hours.
examples.chinese: 仓库里起了火，大火熊熊，不受控制地燃烧了两个多小时。

examples.english: Over the last decade, the government has allowed the spread of poverty and unemployment to continue completely unchecked.
examples.chinese: 在过去十年里，政府任由贫穷和失业继续蔓延，根本不加制止。

expressions.text: charter for somebody to do something

examples.english: This housing law would be a charter for dishonest landlords to cheat their tenants.
examples.chinese: 这条住房法规就等于容许不诚实的房东欺骗租客的通行证。

examples.english: The police decision to reduce their burglary squad has been described as ‘a thieves’ charter'.
examples.chinese: 警方减少防盗分队警力的决定被人称之为“窃贼的特许状”。

centers.english: to let someone do whatever they want
centers.chinese: 让某人想做什么就做什么

expressions.text: give somebody the freedom to do something

examples.english: The children at the school are given complete freedom to study whatever subjects they want.
examples.chinese: 本校的学生想学什么科目是完全自主的。

examples.english: The major argument for capitalism is that it gives people freedom to make their own choices about what they do.
examples.chinese: 资本主义的主要论点是，它可以让人民自由选择做什么。

expressions.text: give somebody free rein/give somebody a free hand

examples.english: We're giving the medical center a free hand as to how it spends the money.
examples.chinese: 至于经费如何使用，我们放手让医疗中心自行决定。

examples.english: The new chairman has been given a completely free hand to make whatever changes he thinks necessary.
examples.chinese: 新主席已被给予充分的自由，可以进行任何他认为是必要的调整。

examples.english: The General seems to have been given carte blanche to attack whatever civilian targets he wants.
examples.chinese: 将军似乎获授予全权，可以随意袭击任何平民目标。

examples.english: The Supreme Court's decision practically gives the police carte blanche to order everyone out when they stop a car.
examples.chinese: 最高法院的判决实际上是给警察部门授予了全权，允许他们拦截汽车时可命令车上的人全部下车。

expressions.text: give somebody a blank cheque /give somebody a blank chec

examples.english: It no longer makes sense to give the Pentagon a blank check when funding for domestic programs is being reduced.
examples.chinese: 国内项目的拨款正在减少，给五角大楼财政全权就不再合理了。

examples.english: We cannot let our democracy become a matter of simply giving a bunch of politicians a blank cheque to govern us every five years.
examples.chinese: 我们不能让我们的民主变成这样——仅仅是给一群政客全权，让他们五年换一届随心所欲地统治我们。

examples.english: Gary and Kaye were often out at work till late evening, but gave us the run of the house.
examples.chinese: 加里和凯经常工作至很晚才回来，但是他们让我们自由进出他们的屋子。

examples.english: He was given the run of Shaw's library while writing his biography.
examples.chinese: 他给萧伯纳写传记期间，可以随意出入他的图书室。

centers.english: to let someone do something that is not usually allowed
centers.chinese: 让某人做一般不允许做的事情

examples.english: The state government was willing to bend the rules where necessary in order to create more jobs.
examples.chinese: 为了创造更多的就业机会，州政府必要时也会通融一下。

examples.english: I'll try and get the housing department to bend the rules for us.
examples.chinese: 我去试试让房屋署给我们通融一下。

examples.english: I'll make an exception this time, but next time you hand in an essay late I won't accept it.
examples.chinese: 这次我就破个例，但是下次你再迟交论文，我就不收了。

examples.english: Under the previous administration, rich landowners were given generous tax concessions.
examples.chinese: 在前一届政府的管治下，富有的地主阶层享有很大的税务优惠。

examples.english: Pensioners and disabled people get special concessions on buses and trains.
examples.chinese: 领取退休金的人士和残疾人士乘坐公共汽车和火车有优惠。

examples.english: Can I be excused from swimming today? I've got a cold.
examples.chinese: 今天我可以不去游泳吗？我感冒了。

examples.english: Kinney asked to be excused from his duties on the board.
examples.chinese: 金尼请求免去他在董事会中的职责。

examples.english: I'll try to get them to excuse me from the meeting.
examples.chinese: 我要想办法让他们不叫我去开会。

examples.english: The new law exempts people who earn less than $8000 a year from paying any taxes.
examples.chinese: 新法律使年收入低于8，000美元的人可以免交任何税金。

examples.english: My father was exempted from military service on the grounds of ill health.
examples.chinese: 我父亲因为健康不佳而免服兵役。

examples.english: The court decided to waive her fine as it was her first offence.
examples.chinese: 因为是初犯，法庭判她免交罚金。

examples.english: The industry asked the Federal Communications Commission to waive a rule that limits the amount of power used to send a data transmission over a telephone line.
examples.chinese: 该行业要求联邦通讯委员会取消限制电话线数据传输功率的规定。

centers.english: official permission to do something

examples.english: I had to get official permission to visit the prison.
examples.chinese: 我必须取得官方许可才能探访这个监狱。

examples.english: We're not allowed to camp here without the farmer's permission.
examples.chinese: 没有农场主的许可，我们不能在此露营。

examples.english: The changes to the book were all made with the author's permission.
examples.chinese: 书中所有改动都得到了作者的许可。

examples.english: I must get authorization from your bank before I can accept a cheque for over fifty pounds.
examples.chinese: 我必须得到你银行的授权，才可接受超过50英镑的支票。

examples.english: As a result of growing world tensions, the army requested authorization to establish another base at Battery Cove.
examples.chinese: 由于世界局势日趋紧张，军队要求准许在炮台湾再建立一个基地。

examples.english: The young couple were married without their parents’ consent.
examples.chinese: 这对年轻人未经父母同意就结婚了。

examples.english: The city authorities have given their consent to leases on two buildings in the centre of Moscow.
examples.chinese: 市政当局已经同意租借莫斯科市中心的两幢大楼。

examples.english: Before a woman can have an abortion, she needs to have written consent from two doctors.
examples.chinese: 妇女堕胎之前需要有两名医生的书面同意。

examples.english: We expect that we'll have clearance from the Justice Department for the buyout in the first quarter of the year.
examples.chinese: 我们希望第一季度的收购行动能得到司法部的批准。

examples.english: The pilot requested clearance to land at Narita Airport.
examples.chinese: 飞行员要求准许在成田机场降落。

examples.english: Kathleen evidently ran things around the office and nothing could be done without her say-so.
examples.chinese: 办公室里的事情显然是凯瑟琳管的，没有她点头同意，什么事也办不了。

examples.english: Tell him he can use the car, but only on my say-so.
examples.chinese: 告诉他可以用车，但只有在我同意之后。

examples.english: The Secret Service seems to think they have a license to tap anyone's phone in the interests of ‘national security.’
examples.chinese: 特工处似乎认为，为了“国家安全”，他们可以窃听任何人的电话。

examples.english: Some manufacturers see the current labelling regulations as licence to mislead shoppers.
examples.chinese: 一些制造商把现行的标签法规当作误导消费者的许可证。

centers.english: an official document that gives permission

examples.english: You can't park here unless you have a permit.
examples.chinese: 你如果没有许可证，不可在此停车。

examples.english: Many spouses of diplomats are unable to pursue their careers because they lack U.S. work permits.
examples.chinese: 许多外交官的配偶因为没有美国的工作许可证，无法追求自己的事业。

examples.english: Do you have a licence for that gun?
examples.chinese: 那支枪你有持枪许可证吗？

examples.english: Rebecca's disability prevented her from getting a driver's license.
examples.chinese: 丽贝卡身体有残疾，使她不能取得驾驶证。

examples.english: A warrant has been issued for the arrest of a suspected terrorist.
examples.chinese: 一名恐怖分子嫌疑犯的逮捕令已经签发。
